text.NRSV: In the thirtieth year, in the fourth month, on the fifth day of the month, as I was among the exiles by the river Chebar, the heavens were opened, and I saw visions of God.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass in the thirtieth year, in the fourth month on the fifth day of the month I being in the midst of them of the captivity, by the river Chebar, that the heavens were opened, and I saw visions of God.

text.NRSV: On the fifth day of the month (it was the fifth year of the exile of King Jehoiachin),
text.Rotherham: On the fifth of the month, that was the fifth year of the captivity of King Jehoiachin,

text.NRSV: the word of the Lord came to the priest Ezekiel son of Buzi, in the land of the Chaldeans by the river Chebar; and the hand of the Lord was on him there.
text.Rotherham: in very deed came the word of Yahweh unto Ezekiel son of Buzi the priest in the land of the Chaldeans by the river Chebar,—yea the hand of Yahweh, came on him there.

text.NRSV: As I looked, a stormy wind came out of the north: a great cloud with brightness around it and fire flashing forth continually, and in the middle of the fire, something like gleaming amber.
text.Rotherham: So then I beheld and lo! a tempestuous wind coming out of the North, a great cloud and a fire catching hold of itself, and it had a brightness round about,—and out of the midst thereof, as burnished copper to look upon out of the midst of the fire;

text.NRSV: In the middle of it was something like four living creatures. This was their appearance: they were of human form.
text.Rotherham: and out of the midst thereof, a likeness of four living ones, and this was their appearance, the likeness of a man, had they;

text.NRSV: Each had four faces, and each of them had four wings.
text.Rotherham: and four faces each, and four wings each, had they;

text.NRSV: Their legs were straight, and the soles of their feet were like the sole of a calf’s foot; and they sparkled like burnished bronze.
text.Rotherham: and their feet were straight feet,—and the sole of their feet was like the sole of the foot of a calf, but sparkling, as shining bronze to look upon;

text.NRSV: Under their wings on their four sides they had human hands. And the four had their faces and their wings thus:
text.Rotherham: and the hands of a man! were under their wings, on their four sides,—and their faces and their wings belonged to the four of them:

text.NRSV: their wings touched one another; each of them moved straight ahead, without turning as they moved.
text.Rotherham: joined to each other, were their wings,—they turned not when they went, each one straight before him, did they go;

text.NRSV: As for the appearance of their faces: the four had the face of a human being, the face of a lion on the right side, the face of an ox on the left side, and the face of an eagle.
text.Rotherham: and the likeness of their faces was the face of a man, with the face of a lion to the right of the four of them, and the face of an ox on the left, of the four of them,—the face of an eagle, also had they four;

text.NRSV: Such were their faces. Their wings were spread out above; each creature had two wings, each of which touched the wing of another, while two covered their bodies.
text.Rotherham: and their wings were spread above,— two of each joined one another, and two covered their bodies;

text.NRSV: Each moved straight ahead; wherever the spirit would go, they went, without turning as they went.
text.Rotherham: and each one straight before him, did they go,—whithersoever the spirit was minded to go, they went, they turned not when they went.

text.NRSV: In the middle of the living creatures there was something that looked like burning coals of fire, like torches moving to and fro among the living creatures; the fire was bright, and lightning issued from the fire.
text.Rotherham: As for the likeness of the living ones, their appearance was like live coals of fire burning up like the appearance of torches, the same went to and fro between the living ones,—and the fire had brightness and out of the fire, went forth a lightning.

text.NRSV: The living creatures darted to and fro, like a flash of lightning.
text.Rotherham: And the living ones ran and returned,—like the appearance of a flash of lightning.

text.NRSV: As I looked at the living creatures, I saw a wheel on the earth beside the living creatures, one for each of the four of them.
text.Rotherham: And I looked at the living ones,—and lo! one wheel upon the earth beside the living ones for each of his four faces,

text.NRSV: As for the appearance of the wheels and their construction: their appearance was like the gleaming of beryl; and the four had the same form, their construction being something like a wheel within a wheel.
text.Rotherham: The appearance of the wheels and of their structure was like a Tarshish-stone to look upon, and they four had one likeness,—and their appearance and their structure were just as would be a wheel in the midst of a wheel.

text.NRSV: When they moved, they moved in any of the four directions without veering as they moved.
text.Rotherham: Upon their four sides, when they went, so did they go,—they turned not when they went.

text.NRSV: Their rims were tall and awesome, for the rims of all four were full of eyes all around.
text.Rotherham: As for their rings, they were so high that they were terrible, -and their rings full of eyes round about, had they four.

text.NRSV: When the living creatures moved, the wheels moved beside them; and when the living creatures rose from the earth, the wheels rose.
text.Rotherham: And when the living ones went, the wheels went beside them,—and when the living ones were lifted up from the earth, the wheels were lifted up:

text.NRSV: Wherever the spirit would go, they went, and the wheels rose along with them; for the spirit of the living creatures was in the wheels.
text.Rotherham: whithersoever the spirit was minded to go, they went—thither was the spirit minded to go,—and the wheels would be lifted up along with them, for the spirit of the living one was in the wheels:

text.NRSV: When they moved, the others moved; when they stopped, the others stopped; and when they rose from the earth, the wheels rose along with them; for the spirit of the living creatures was in the wheels.
text.Rotherham: when they went, these went, and when they stood, these stood,—and when they were lifted up from off the earth, the wheels were lifted up along with them, for the spirit of the living one was in the wheels.

text.NRSV: Over the heads of the living creatures there was something like a dome, shining like fearful ice, spread out over their heads above.
text.Rotherham: And there was a likeness over the heads of the living one—an expanse, like terrible crystal to look upon, -stretched forth over their heads above.

text.NRSV: And under the dome their wings were stretched out straight, one toward another; and each of the creatures had two wings covering its body.
text.Rotherham: And under the expanse, their wings were straight, one towards another,—each one had two covering on this side, and each one had two covering on that side, their bodies.

text.NRSV: When they moved, I heard the sound of their wings like the roar of mighty waters, like the thunder of the Almighty, a sound of tumult like the sound of an army; when they stopped, they let down their wings.
text.Rotherham: And I heard the sound of their wings, as the sound of many waters, as the sound of the Almighty, when they went, the sound of a storm as the sound of a host,—when they stood, they let down their wings,

text.NRSV: And there came a voice from above the dome over their heads; when they stopped, they let down their wings.
text.Rotherham: And there was a voice above the expanse that was over their head,—when they stood, they let down their wings.

text.NRSV: And above the dome over their heads there was something like a throne, in appearance like sapphire; and seated above the likeness of a throne was something that seemed like a human form.
text.Rotherham: And above the expanse that was over their heads, as the appearance of a sapphire stone, was the likeness of a throne,—and upon the likeness of a throne, was a likeness as the appearance of a man upon it above.

text.NRSV: Upward from what appeared like the loins I saw something like gleaming amber, something that looked like fire enclosed all around; and downward from what appeared like the loins I saw something that looked like fire, and there was a splendor all around.
text.Rotherham: And I saw as burnished copper to look upon, as the appearance of fire within it round about, from the appearance of his loins and upwards and from the appearance of his loins and downwards, saw I as the appearance of fire, and he had brightness round about:

text.NRSV: Like the bow in a cloud on a rainy day, such was the appearance of the splendor all around. This was the appearance of the likeness of the glory of the Lord. When I saw it, I fell on my face, and I heard the voice of someone speaking.
text.Rotherham: as the appearance of the bow which is in a cloud on a day of rain, so was the appearance of the brightness round about, that was the appearance of the likeness of the glory of Yahweh, and when I saw, I fell upon my face, and heard a voice of one speaking.

text.NRSV: He said to me: O mortal, stand up on your feet, and I will speak with you.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto me,—Son of man, Stand upon thy feet, that I may, speak with thee.

text.NRSV: And when he spoke to me, a spirit entered into me and set me on my feet; and I heard him speaking to me.
text.Rotherham: Then the Spirit entered into me as soon as he spake unto me, and it caused me to stand upon my feet,—and I heard one speaking unto me.

text.NRSV: He said to me, Mortal, I am sending you to the people of Israel, to a nation of rebels who have rebelled against me; they and their ancestors have transgressed against me to this very day.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto me- Son of man I am sending thee unto the sons of Israel, unto rebellious nations, which have rebelled against me,—they, and their fathers have transgressed against me, until this very day;

text.NRSV: The descendants are impudent and stubborn. I am sending you to them, and you shall say to them, “Thus says the Lord God.”
text.Rotherham: and it is unto such sons—of shameless face, and emboldened heart, that I am sending thee,—therefore shalt thou say unto them, Thus saith My Lord, Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Whether they hear or refuse to hear (for they are a rebellious house), they shall know that there has been a prophet among them.
text.Rotherham: And they! whether they will hear or whether they will forbear, for a perverse house, they are, shall then know that, a prophet, hath been in their midst.

text.NRSV: And you, O mortal, do not be afraid of them, and do not be afraid of their words, though briers and thorns surround you and you live among scorpions; do not be afraid of their words, and do not be dismayed at their looks, for they are a rebellious house.
text.Rotherham: But thou son of man Be not afraid of them Nor of their words, be afraid Though thorns and thistles are about thee, And amongst prickly plants, thou dost dwell, Of their words, be not afraid nor At their faces, be thou dismayed, For a perverse house, they are!

text.NRSV: You shall speak my words to them, whether they hear or refuse to hear; for they are a rebellious house.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt therefore speak my words unto them, whether they will hear, or whether they will forbear For perverse, they are!

text.NRSV: But you, mortal, hear what I say to you; do not be rebellious like that rebellious house; open your mouth and eat what I give you.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore son of man, hear what I am speaking unto thee., become not perverse like the perverse house,—open thy mouth, and eat that which I am giving unto thee.

text.NRSV: I looked, and a hand was stretched out to me, and a written scroll was in it.
text.Rotherham: So I looked, and lo! a hand put forth unto me,—and lo! therein a scroll;

text.NRSV: He spread it before me; it had writing on the front and on the back, and written on it were words of lamentation and mourning and woe.
text.Rotherham: and he spread it out before me, and, it was written on the front and the back,—and there were written thereon, lamentation and sighing, and wailing.

text.NRSV: He said to me, Mortal, eat what is offered to you; eat this scroll, and go, speak to the house of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me, Son of man What thou findest, eat,—eat this roll, and go speak unto the house of Israel.

text.NRSV: So I opened my mouth, and he gave me the scroll to eat.
text.Rotherham: So I opened my mouth,—and he caused me to eat this roll.

text.NRSV: He said to me, Mortal, eat this scroll that I give you and fill your stomach with it. Then I ate it; and in my mouth it was as sweet as honey.
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me Son of man. Thy belly, cause thou to eat and thy bowels, fill thou with this roll which I am giving unto thee. So I did eat, and it became in my mouth, as honey for sweetness.

text.NRSV: He said to me: Mortal, go to the house of Israel and speak my words to them.
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me,— Son of man Go get thee unto the house of Israel, and thou shalt speak with my words, unto them.

text.NRSV: For you are not sent to a people of obscure speech and difficult language, but to the house of Israel—
text.Rotherham: For not unto a people deep of lip and heavy of tongue, art thou sent —[but] unto the house of Israel:

text.NRSV: not to many peoples of obscure speech and difficult language, whose words you cannot understand. Surely, if I sent you to them, they would listen to you.
text.Rotherham: not unto many peoples deep of lip and heavy of tongue, whose words thou couldst not understand,—surely if unto them I had sent thee, they would have hearkened unto thee.

text.NRSV: But the house of Israel will not listen to you, for they are not willing to listen to me; because all the house of Israel have a hard forehead and a stubborn heart.
text.Rotherham: But the house of Israel will not be willing to hearken unto thee, for they are not willing to hearken unto me,—for all the house of Israel are bold of forehead and hard of heart.

text.NRSV: See, I have made your face as hard as their faces, and your forehead as hard as their foreheads.
text.Rotherham: Lo! I have made thy face bold like as their faces, and thy forehead bold like as their forehead.

text.NRSV: Like adamant, harder than flint, I have made your forehead; do not fear them or be dismayed at their looks, for they are a rebellious house.
text.Rotherham: As an adamant harder than flint, have I made thy forehead,—thou shalt not fear them neither shalt thou be dismayed at their faces, For a perverse house, they are!

text.NRSV: He said to me: Mortal, all my words that I shall speak to you receive in your heart and hear with your ears;
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me,— Son of man. All my words which I shall speak unto thee, receive thou into thy heart and in thine ears, hear thou:

text.NRSV: then go to the exiles, to your people, and speak to them. Say to them, “Thus says the Lord God”; whether they hear or refuse to hear.
text.Rotherham: then go get thee unto them of the captivity, unto the sons of thy people, and thou shalt speak unto them and say unto them, Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, whether they will hear or whether they will forbear.

text.NRSV: Then the spirit lifted me up, and as the glory of the Lord rose from its place, I heard behind me the sound of loud rumbling;
text.Rotherham: Then the Spirit lifted me up, and I heard behind me the sound of a great rushing,— Blessed be the glory of Yahweh. From his place!

text.NRSV: it was the sound of the wings of the living creatures brushing against one another, and the sound of the wheels beside them, that sounded like a loud rumbling.
text.Rotherham: Yea the sound of the wings of the living ones, gently touching each other, and the sound of the wheels, along with them, even the sound of a great rushing.

text.NRSV: The spirit lifted me up and bore me away; I went in bitterness in the heat of my spirit, the hand of the Lord being strong upon me.
text.Rotherham: So the Spirit lifted me up, and took me away,—and I went bitterly, in the rage of my spirit, but the hand of Yahweh upon me was strong.

text.NRSV: I came to the exiles at Tel-abib, who lived by the river Chebar. And I sat there among them, stunned, for seven days.
text.Rotherham: Thus came I unto them of the captivity, at Tel-abib. who were dwelling towards he river Chebar and I dwelt where They were dwelling; yea I dwelt there seven days, stunned in their midst,

text.NRSV: At the end of seven days, the word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass at the end of seven days, that the word of Yahweh came unto me saying

text.NRSV: Mortal, I have made you a sentinel for the house of Israel; whenever you hear a word from my mouth, you shall give them warning from me.
text.Rotherham: Son of man, A watchman, have I appointed thee to the house of Israel,—and thou shalt hear, at my mouth a message, and shalt warn them from me.

text.NRSV: If I say to the wicked, “You shall surely die,” and you give them no warning, or speak to warn the wicked from their wicked way, in order to save their life, those wicked persons shall die for their iniquity; but their blood I will require at your hand.
text.Rotherham: When I say to the lawless man— Thou shalt surely die, and thou hast not given him warning, neither hast spoken to warn the lawless man from his lawless way, to save himself alive the same lawless man in his iniquity shall die, but his blood, at thy hand, will I require.

text.NRSV: But if you warn the wicked, and they do not turn from their wickedness, or from their wicked way, they shall die for their iniquity; but you will have saved your life.
text.Rotherham: Whereas when thou hast warned a lawless man, and he hath not turned from his lawlessness and from his lawless way he in his iniquity shall die, but Thou hast delivered thine own soul.

text.NRSV: Again, if the righteous turn from their righteousness and commit iniquity, and I lay a stumbling block before them, they shall die; because you have not warned them, they shall die for their sin, and their righteous deeds that they have done shall not be remembered; but their blood I will require at your hand.
text.Rotherham: And when a righteous man hath turned from his righteousness, and committed perversity, and I have suffered a stumbling- block to be laid before him he shall die,—though thou hast not warned him, in his sin, shall he die, neither shall be remembered his righteous deeds which he hath done, but his blood, at thy hand will I require;

text.NRSV: If, however, you warn the righteous not to sin, and they do not sin, they shall surely live, because they took warning; and you will have saved your life.
text.Rotherham: whereas when Thou hast warned a righteous man. That a righteous man must not sin and he hath not sinned, he shall surely live in that he took warning, and thou hast delivered thine own soul.

text.NRSV: Then the hand of the Lord was upon me there; and he said to me, Rise up, go out into the valley, and there I will speak with you.
text.Rotherham: Then came upon me there the hand of Yahweh,—and he said unto me, Arise go forth into the valley, and there, will I speak with thee.

text.NRSV: So I rose up and went out into the valley; and the glory of the Lord stood there, like the glory that I had seen by the river Chebar; and I fell on my face.
text.Rotherham: So I arose and went forth into the valley, and lo! there the glory of Yahweh, standing, like the glory which I saw by the river Chebar,-and I fell upon my face.

text.NRSV: But a spirit entered into me, and set me on my feet; and he spoke with me and said to me: Go, shut yourself inside your house.
text.Rotherham: Then the Spirit entered into me, and caused me to stand upon my feet,—and he spake with me and said unto me, Go in shut thyself up, in the midst of thine own house,

text.NRSV: As for you, mortal, cords shall be placed on you, and you shall be bound with them, so that you cannot go out among the people;
text.Rotherham: And thou—O son of man, lo! they have put upon thee bands, and they will bind thee therewith,-.so that thou go not forth in their midst;

text.NRSV: and I will make your tongue cling to the roof of your mouth, so that you shall be speechless and unable to reprove them; for they are a rebellious house.
text.Rotherham: thy tongue also, will I cause to cleave unto the roof of thy mouth, so shalt thou be dumb and not be to them a reprover,— For a perverse house, they are!

text.NRSV: But when I speak with you, I will open your mouth, and you shall say to them, “Thus says the Lord God”; let those who will hear, hear; and let those who refuse to hear, refuse; for they are a rebellious house.
text.Rotherham: But when I speak with thee, I will open thy mouth, and thou shalt say unto them. Thus saith My Lord Yahweh,—He that is minded to hear, let him hear, And he that is minded to forbear, let him forbear! For a perverse house, they are!

text.NRSV: And you, O mortal, take a brick and set it before you. On it portray a city, Jerusalem;
text.Rotherham: Thou therefore O son of man Take thee a tile, and lay it before thee,—and portray thereon a city, even Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: and put siegeworks against it, and build a siege-wall against it, and cast up a mound against it; set camps also against it, and plant battering rams against it all around.
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou lay siege against it And build up against it a siege-wall And cast up against it, a mound,— And set against it camps, And place against it battering-rams, round about.

text.NRSV: Then take an iron plate and place it as an iron wall between you and the city; set your face toward it, and let it be in a state of siege, and press the siege against it. This is a sign for the house of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore take thee a pan of iron, and set it for a wall of iron, between thee and the city,—then shalt thou direct thy face against it and it shall come into siege and thou shalt lay siege to it, a sign, shall it be to the house of Israel.

text.NRSV: Then lie on your left side, and place the punishment of the house of Israel upon it; you shall bear their punishment for the number of days that you lie on it.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore lie thou on thy left side, and lay the iniquity of the house of Israel upon it,— during the number of the days which thou shalt lie thereon, shalt thou bear their punishment.

text.NRSV: For I assign to you a number of days, three hundred ninety days, equal to the number of the years of their punishment; and so you shall bear the punishment of the house of Israel.
text.Rotherham: I therefore have appointed thee the years of their punishment, by the number of days a hundred and ninety days,—so shalt thou bear the iniquity of the house of Israel.

text.NRSV: When you have completed these, you shall lie down a second time, but on your right side, and bear the punishment of the house of Judah; forty days I assign you, one day for each year.
text.Rotherham: And when thou hast ended these, then shalt thou lie, on thy right side. a second time, and shalt bear the iniquity of the house of Judah,— forty days, one day for each year, have I appointed thee.

text.NRSV: You shall set your face toward the siege of Jerusalem, and with your arm bared you shall prophesy against it.
text.Rotherham: Thus then unto the siege of Jerusalem, shalt thou direct thy face, with thine arm made bare,—so shalt thou prophesy against it.

text.NRSV: See, I am putting cords on you so that you cannot turn from one side to the other until you have completed the days of your siege.
text.Rotherham: And lo! I have laid upon thee-bands,—and thou shalt not turn thee from thy one side to thine other, until thou have ended the days of thy siege.

text.NRSV: And you, take wheat and barley, beans and lentils, millet and spelt; put them into one vessel, and make bread for yourself. During the number of days that you lie on your side, three hundred ninety days, you shall eat it.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, take thee wheat and barley and peas and lentils and millet and spelt, and put them in one vessel, and make them ready for thee as bread,—during the number of days which thou art lying on thy side. a hundred and ninety days, shalt thou eat it;

text.NRSV: The food you eat shall be twenty shekels a day by weight; at fixed times you shall eat it.
text.Rotherham: and thy food which thou shalt eat shall be by weight twenty shekels a day,— from time to time, shalt thou eat it;

text.NRSV: And you shall drink water by measure, one-sixth of a hin; at fixed times you shall drink it.
text.Rotherham: and water by measure, shalt thou drink the sixth part of a hin,-from time to time shalt thou drink;

text.NRSV: You shall eat it as a barley-cake, baking it in their sight on human dung.
text.Rotherham: and as a barley cake, shalt thou eat it,—and the same with dung proceeding from man, shalt thou bake, before their eyes.

text.NRSV: The Lord said, “Thus shall the people of Israel eat their bread, unclean, among the nations to which I will drive them.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh, In like manner, shall the sons of Israel eat their bread defiled,—among the nations whither I will drive them.

text.NRSV: Then I said, “Ah, Lord God! I have never defiled myself; from my youth up until now I have never eaten what died of itself or was torn by animals, nor has carrion flesh come into my mouth.”
text.Rotherham: Then said, I, Ah! My Lord. Yahweh, lo! my person, hath not been defiled: neither of what hath died of itself nor of what hath been torn in pieces: have I eaten, from my youthful days even unto now, neither hath come into my mouth the flesh of a foul thing.

text.NRSV: Then he said to me, “See, I will let you have cow’s dung instead of human dung, on which you may prepare your bread.”
text.Rotherham: So then he said unto me, See I have granted thee cow’s dung for man’s dung,—and thou shalt prepare thy bread thereupon.

text.NRSV: Then he said to me, Mortal, I am going to break the staff of bread in Jerusalem; they shall eat bread by weight and with fearfulness; and they shall drink water by measure and in dismay.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto me, Son of man Behold me! breaking the staff of bread in Jerusalem, So shall they eat bread by weight and with anxious care, And water by measure, and in astonishment, shall they drink:

text.NRSV: Lacking bread and water, they will look at one another in dismay, and waste away under their punishment.
text.Rotherham: that they may lack bread and water and be astonished one with another, and pine away in their punishment.

text.NRSV: And you, O mortal, take a sharp sword; use it as a barber’s razor and run it over your head and your beard; then take balances for weighing, and divide the hair.
text.Rotherham: Thou therefore son of man Take thee a sharp cutting instrument, a barber’s razor, shalt thou take thee, and shalt cause it to pass upon thy head and upon thy chin,—and shalt take the balances for weighing, and shalt divide the hair.

text.NRSV: One third you shall burn in the fire inside the city, when the days of the siege are completed; one third you shall take and strike with the sword all around the city; and one third you shall scatter to the wind, and I will unsheathe the sword after them.
text.Rotherham: A third part in the fire, shalt thou burn in the midst of the city, when the days of the siege are fulfilled,—and thou shalt take a third part, thou shalt smite it with the cuting instrument round about it, and a third part, shalt thou scatter to the wind, and, a sword, will I, make bare after them;

text.NRSV: Then you shall take from these a small number and bind them in the skirts of your robe.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt take from thence, a few in number,—and shalt bind them in thy skirts;

text.NRSV: From these, again, you shall take some and throw them into the fire and burn them up; from there a fire will come out against all the house of Israel.
text.Rotherham: and of them, again take, and cast them into the midst of the fire, and burn them in the fire,-therefrom, shall go forth a fire unto all the house of Israel.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: This is Jerusalem; I have set her in the center of the nations, with countries all around her.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith My Lord, Yahweh, This, is Jerusalem, In the midst of the nations, I placed her,— and of the countries round about her;

text.NRSV: But she has rebelled against my ordinances and my statutes, becoming more wicked than the nations and the countries all around her, rejecting my ordinances and not following my statutes.
text.Rotherham: But she hath changed my regulations into lawlessness, more than the nations, And my statutes more than the countries which are round bout her, For my regulations, have they rejected, And in my statutes, have they not walked.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God: Because you are more turbulent than the nations that are all around you, and have not followed my statutes or kept my ordinances, but have acted according to the ordinances of the nations that are all around you;
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus saith My Lord, Yahweh. Because ye have become more rebellious than the nations who are round about you, In my statutes, have not walked. And my regulations, have not done, And according to the regulations of the nations which are round about you, have not done:

text.NRSV: therefore thus says the Lord God: I, I myself, am coming against you; I will execute judgments among you in the sight of the nations.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Lo! I am against thee, even I, Therefore will I, execute in thy midst, judgments, in the eyes of the nations;

text.NRSV: And because of all your abominations, I will do to you what I have never yet done, and the like of which I will never do again.
text.Rotherham: Yea I will execute upon thee that which I have not executed, And like unto which I will not execute again,- Because of all thine abominations.

text.NRSV: Surely, parents shall eat their children in your midst, and children shall eat their parents; I will execute judgments on you, and any of you who survive I will scatter to every wind.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, fathers will eat sons in thy midst, And sons will eat fathers,— And I will execute upon thee judgments, And will scatter the whole remnant of thee, to every wind.

text.NRSV: Therefore, as I live, says the Lord God, surely, because you have defiled my sanctuary with all your detestable things and with all your abominations—therefore I will cut you down; my eye will not spare, and I will have no pity.
text.Rotherham: Therefore as I live, Declareth My Lord Yahweh,— Surely because my sanctuary, thou hast defiled, with all thy detestable things, and with all thine abominations Even I also, will cut off, and mine eye, shall not spare, Yea even I will not pity.

text.NRSV: One third of you shall die of pestilence or be consumed by famine among you; one third shall fall by the sword around you; and one third I will scatter to every wind and will unsheathe the sword after them.
text.Rotherham: The third part of thee by pestilence, shall die, and by famine, be consumed in thy midst And the third part by the sword, shall fall round about thee,- And the third part to every wind, will I scatter, and a sword, will I make bare after them.

text.NRSV: My anger shall spend itself, and I will vent my fury on them and satisfy myself; and they shall know that I, the Lord, have spoken in my jealousy, when I spend my fury on them.
text.Rotherham: So shall mine anger have an end, And I will cause mine indignation, to find rest, upon them, and will console myself,- And they shall know that, I, Yahweh, have spoken in my jealousy, when mine indignation hath come to an end, upon them.

text.NRSV: Moreover I will make you a desolation and an object of mocking among the nations around you, in the sight of all that pass by.
text.Rotherham: And I will deliver thee up to desolation and reproach, among the nations that are round about thee,—in the eyes of every passer-by.

text.NRSV: You shall be a mockery and a taunt, a warning and a horror to the nations around you, when I execute judgments on you in anger and fury, and with furious punishments—I, the Lord, have spoken.
text.Rotherham: So shall it become a reproach and an insult a warning and an astonishment, to the nations that are round about thee,—in that I have executed upon thee judgments in anger and in indignation and in rebukes of indignation, I, Yahweh have spoken:

text.NRSV: When I loose against you my deadly arrows of famine, arrows for destruction, which I will let loose to destroy you, and when I bring more and more famine upon you, and break your staff of bread,
text.Rotherham: In that I have sent the malignant arrows of famine among them which were for destruction, which I will send to destroy you,—and famine, will I increase upon you, and will break for you the staff of bread.

text.NRSV: I will send famine and wild animals against you, and they will rob you of your children; pestilence and bloodshed shall pass through you; and I will bring the sword upon you. I, the Lord, have spoken.
text.Rotherham: So will I send upon you famine and mischievous beast and they shall bereave thee,—and pestilence and blood, shall pass through thee,—and a sword, will I bring in upon thee, I Yahweh, have spoken.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh. unto me saying,

text.NRSV: O mortal, set your face toward the mountains of Israel, and prophesy against them,
text.Rotherham: Son of man, Set thy face against the mountains of Israel,— and prophesy unto them;

text.NRSV: and say, You mountains of Israel, hear the word of the Lord God! Thus says the Lord God to the mountains and the hills, to the ravines and the valleys: I, I myself will bring a sword upon you, and I will destroy your high places.
text.Rotherham: and say, Ye mountains of Israel, hear ye the word of My Lord Yahweh,— Thus saith My Lord, Yahweh- To the mountains and to the hills to the hollows and to the valleys,- Behold me! I, am bringing upon you a sword, And I will destroy your high places;

text.NRSV: Your altars shall become desolate, and your incense-stands shall be broken; and I will throw down your slain in front of your idols.
text.Rotherham: And your altars shall be laid waste, And your sun-pillars shall be broken in pieces,— And I will cause your slain to fall before your manufactured gods;

text.NRSV: I will lay the corpses of the people of Israel in front of their idols; and I will scatter your bones around your altars.
text.Rotherham: And I will lay the carcases of the sons of Israel before their manufactured gods,— And will scatter your bones round about your altars.

text.NRSV: Wherever you live, your towns shall be waste and your high places ruined, so that your altars will be waste and ruined, your idols broken and destroyed, your incense-stands cut down, and your works wiped out.
text.Rotherham: In all your dwelling-places, The cities, shall be laid waste, and, The high places, shall be made desolate, To the end your altars may be laid waste and become desolate. And your manufactured gods be broken in pieces and cease, And your sun-pillars be cut down, And your handiworks be abolished.

text.NRSV: The slain shall fall in your midst; then you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And the slain shall fall in your midst, So shall ye know that I am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: But I will spare some. Some of you shall escape the sword among the nations and be scattered through the countries.
text.Rotherham: Yet will I leave a remnant: In that ye shall have such as are escaped of the sword throughout the nations, when ye are scattered throughout the lands.

text.NRSV: Those of you who escape shall remember me among the nations where they are carried captive, how I was crushed by their wanton heart that turned away from me, and by their wanton eyes that turned after their idols. Then they will be loathsome in their own sight for the evils that they have committed, for all their abominations.
text.Rotherham: Then shall they who have escaped of you remember me, among the nations whither they have been carried captive, In that I have broken their adulterous heart, which hath turned aside from me, And their eyes which have adulterously gone after their manufactured gods, So shall they become loathsome in their own sight, for the wicked things which they have done in all their abominations.

text.NRSV: And they shall know that I am the Lord; I did not threaten in vain to bring this disaster upon them.
text.Rotherham: Then shall they know that, I, Yahweh,—not in vain, had threatened to bring upon them this calamity.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: Clap your hands and stamp your foot, and say, Alas! because of all the evil abominations of the house of Israel; for they shall fall by the sword, by famine, and by pestilence.
text.Rotherham: Thus saith My Lord. Yahweh, Smite with thy hand, and stamp with thy foot and say, Alas! regarding all the wicked abominations of the house of Israel,—in that by sword, by famine, and by pestilence, shall they fall:

text.NRSV: Those far off shall die of pestilence; those nearby shall fall by the sword; and any who are left and are spared shall die of famine. Thus I will spend my fury upon them.
text.Rotherham: He that is far off by pestilence, shall die and He that is near by the sword, shall fall, and He that is left and is besieged by the famine, shall die; Thus will I make an end of mine indignation against them.

text.NRSV: And you shall know that I am the Lord, when their slain lie among their idols around their altars, on every high hill, on all the mountain tops, under every green tree, and under every leafy oak, wherever they offered pleasing odor to all their idols.
text.Rotherham: So shall ye know that I, am Yahweh, In that their slain are in the midst of their manufactured gods, on every side of their altars,— Upon every high hill In all the tops of the mountains and Under every green tree and Under every tangled oak, The place where they offered a satisfying odour to all their manufactured gods.

text.NRSV: I will stretch out my hand against them, and make the land desolate and waste, throughout all their settlements, from the wilderness to Riblah. Then they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thus will I stretch out my hand upon them, and make the land a greater waste and devastation than the desert toward Diblah throughout all their dwelling-places,- And they shall know that, I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying:

text.NRSV: You, O mortal, thus says the Lord God to the land of Israel: An end! The end has come upon the four corners of the land.
text.Rotherham: Thou therefore O son of man. Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, To the so of Israel, there is an end,- The end hath come upon the four skirts of the land.

text.NRSV: Now the end is upon you, I will let loose my anger upon you; I will judge you according to your ways, I will punish you for all your abominations.
text.Rotherham: Now is the end upon thee, Therefore will I send mine anger against thee, And judge thee according to thy ways,- And lay upon thee all thine abominations;

text.NRSV: My eye will not spare you, I will have no pity. I will punish you for your ways, while your abominations are among you. Then you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And mine eye shall not shield thee. Neither will I pity,—. For thy ways—upon thee, will I lay. And thine abominations in thy midst, shall be found, So shall ye know that, I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: Disaster after disaster! See, it comes.
text.Rotherham: Thus saith My Lord Yahweh:A calamity, a sole calamity, lo! it hath come.

text.NRSV: An end has come, the end has come. It has awakened against you; see, it comes!
text.Rotherham: An end hath come, Come hath the end It hath roused itself up against thee,— Lo! it hath come.

text.NRSV: Your doom has come to you, O inhabitant of the land. The time has come, the day is near—of tumult, not of reveling on the mountains.
text.Rotherham: The circle hath come round unto thee. O inhabitant of the land,- The time, hath come, The day of consternation, hath drawn near. And not the joyful shout of the mountains.

text.NRSV: Soon I will pour out my fury upon you; I will spend my anger against you. I will judge you according to your ways, and punish you for all your abominations.
text.Rotherham: Now shortly, will I pour out mine indignation upon thee, And will bring to an end mine anger against thee, And will judge thee according to thy ways,- And will lay upon thee all thine abominations.

text.NRSV: My eye will not spare, I will have no pity. I will punish you according to your ways, while your abominations are among you. Then you shall know that it is I the Lord who strike.
text.Rotherham: And mine eye shall not shield thee Neither will I have pity,— According to thy ways, unto thee will I render, And thine abominations, in thy midst shall be found, So shall ye know that I, Yahweh am smiting.

text.NRSV: See, the day! See, it comes! Your doom has gone out. The rod has blossomed, pride has budded.
text.Rotherham: Lo! the day, Lo! it hath come, The circle, hath gone forth, The sceptre, hath blossomed, Insolence hath sprouted:

text.NRSV: Violence has grown into a rod of wickedness. None of them shall remain, not their abundance, not their wealth; no pre-eminence among them.
text.Rotherham: Violence hath risen up as a sceptre of lawlessness, —There are none of them And none of their multitude And none of their throngs And no lamentation over them.

text.NRSV: The time has come, the day draws near; let not the buyer rejoice, nor the seller mourn, for wrath is upon all their multitude.
text.Rotherham: The time hath come. The day hath arrived, The buyer, let him not rejoice, and The seller, let him not mourn,— For indignation, is against all her multitude.

text.NRSV: For the sellers shall not return to what has been sold as long as they remain alive. For the vision concerns all their multitude; it shall not be revoked. Because of their iniquity, none can maintain their life.
text.Rotherham: For the seller, unto that which is to be sold, shall not return, though yet among the living, were their life, for the vision is against all her multitude. He shall not return, And, no man, by his punishment, shall strengthen, his life.

text.NRSV: They have blown the horn and made everything ready; but no one goes to battle, for my wrath is upon all their multitude.
text.Rotherham: They have blown the trumpet, even to make All ready, Yet is there none going to the battle; For mine indignation is against all her multitude.

text.NRSV: The sword is outside, pestilence and famine are inside; those in the open field shall die by the sword; and, him that is in the city, famine and pestilence shall devour.
text.Rotherham: The sword without, and Pestilence and famine within,— He that is in the field by the sword, shall die, and He that is in the city famine or pestilence shall devour him;

text.NRSV: If any survivors escape, they shall be on the mountains like doves of the valleys, all of them moaning over their iniquity.
text.Rotherham: While they who escape of them shall escape and become on the mountains as the doves of the valleys, all of them cooing,—each one in his punishment.

text.NRSV: All hands shall grow feeble, all knees turn to water.
text.Rotherham: All hands shall be unnerved; and All knees shall be weak as water,

text.NRSV: They shall gird themselves with sackcloth, and horror shall cover them; shame shall be on all faces, and baldness on all their heads.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall they gird themselves with sackcloth, And shuddering shall cover them,-And in all faces, shall be paleness, And in all their heads, baldness.

text.NRSV: They shall flung their silver into the streets, and their gold shall be like an unclean thing. Their silver and gold cannot save them on the day of the wrath of the Lord. They shall not satisfy their hunger or fill their stomachs with it. For it was the stumbling block of their iniquity.
text.Rotherham: Their silver into the streets, shall they cast and Their gold for throwing away, shall serve, Their silver and their gold shall not be able to deliver them in the day of the wrath of Yahweh, Their craving, shall they not satisfy, and Their belly, shall they not fill, For a stumbling-block, hath their iniquity become.

text.NRSV: From their beautiful ornament, in which they took pride, they made their abominable images, their detestable things; therefore I will make of it an unclean thing to them.
text.Rotherham: When, the beauty of his own ornament, he had, in majesty, placed, Then, the images, of their abominations Their detestable things, Made they therein,— For this cause have I delivered it up to them for removal;

text.NRSV: I will hand it over to strangers as booty, to the wicked of the earth as plunder; they shall profane it.
text.Rotherham: Yea I will deliver it Into the hand of foreigners for a prey, And to the lawless ones of the earth for a spoil, And they will profane it;

text.NRSV: I will avert my face from them, so that they may profane my treasured place; robbers shall enter it and profane it.
text.Rotherham: And I will turn away my face from them, And they will profane my cherished place,—Yea there will enter it violent men Who will profane it.

text.NRSV: Make a chain! For the land is full of bloody crimes, and the city is full of violence.
text.Rotherham: Prepare thou a chain,— For, the land, is full of the crime of bloodshed, And, the city, is full of violence.

text.NRSV: I will bring the worst of the nations to take possession of their houses. I will put an end to the arrogance of the strong, and their holy places shall be profaned.
text.Rotherham: So then I will bring in the wicked ones of the nations, And they shall take possession of their houses,—And I will cause to cease the pomp of the strong ones, And their holy places shall be profaned.

text.NRSV: When anguish comes, they shall seek peace, but there shall be none.
text.Rotherham: Destruction, hath entered,- And they shall seek welfare and there be none.

text.NRSV: Disaster comes upon disaster, rumor follows rumor; they shall keep seeking a vision from the prophet; instruction shall perish from the priest, and counsel from the elders.
text.Rotherham: Disaster upon disaster, shall come, And report after report shall arise, Therefore shall they seek a vision from prophet, And law shall perish from priest. And counsel from elders.

text.NRSV: The king shall mourn, the prince shall be clothed with desolation, and the hands of the people of the land shall tremble. According to their way I will deal with them; according to their own judgments I will judge them. Then they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: The king, shall mourn and, The chief, shall clothe himself with astonishment, and, the hands of the people of the land, shall be wrung in anguish,— After their own way, will I deal with them and, With their own sentences, will I sentence them, And they shall know that, I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: In the sixth year, in the sixth month, on the fifth day of the month, as I sat in my house, with the elders of Judah sitting before me, the hand of the Lord God fell upon me there.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in the sixth year in the sixth month, on the fifth of the month, I being seated in my house, and the elders of Judah being seated before me, then fell upon me there the hand of My Lord Yahweh.

text.NRSV: I looked, and there was a figure that looked like a human being; below what appeared to be its loins it was fire, and above the loins it was like the appearance of brightness, like gleaming amber.
text.Rotherham: So I looked, and lo! a likeness as the appearance of a man, from the appearance of his loins and downward fire, and from his loins and upwards as an appearance of shining, as the look of amber,

text.NRSV: It stretched out the form of a hand, and took me by a lock of my head; and the spirit lifted me up between earth and heaven, and brought me in visions of God to Jerusalem, to the entrance of the gateway of the inner court that faces north, where the seat of the image of jealousy, which provokes to jealousy, was located.
text.Rotherham: Then put he forth the similitude of a hand, and took me by the forelock of my head,—and the Spirit lifted me up between the earth and the heavens and brought me to Jerusalem in the visions of God into the opening of the inner gate that looketh toward the north, where was the seat of the Statue of Jealousy. that provoketh to jealousy;

text.NRSV: And the glory of the God of Israel was there, like the vision that I had seen in the valley.
text.Rotherham: and lo! there, the glory of the God of Israel, like the appearance which I had seen in the valley.

text.NRSV: Then he said to me, “O mortal, lift up your eyes now toward the north.” So I lifted up my eyes toward the north, and there, north of the altar gate, in the entrance, was this image of jealousy.
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me, Son of man Lift up. I pray thee thine eyes, the way toward the north. So I lifted up mine eyes the way toward the north, and lo! on the north by the gate of the altar, this Statue of Jealousy, in the entrance.

text.NRSV: He said to me, “Mortal, do you see what they are doing, the great abominations that the house of Israel are committing here, to drive me far from my sanctuary? Yet you will see still greater abominations.”
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me, Son of man Canst thou see what they are doing,—the great abominations which the house of Israel are committing here, that I should go faraway from my sanctuary? Howbeit yet again, shalt thou see great abominations.

text.NRSV: And he brought me to the entrance of the court; I looked, and there was a hole in the wall.
text.Rotherham: So he brought me into the opening of the court,—and I looked, and lo! a single hole in the wall.

text.NRSV: Then he said to me, “Mortal, dig through the wall”; and when I digged through the wall, there was a door.
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me Son of man Break I pray thee through the wall. So I broke through the wall, and lo! a single opening.

text.NRSV: He said to me, “Go in, and see the vile abominations that they are committing here.”
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me,— Go in, and see the wicked abominations. which they are committing here.

text.NRSV: So I went in and looked; there, portrayed on the wall all around, were all kinds of creeping things, and loathsome animals, and all the idols of the house of Israel.
text.Rotherham: So I went in. and looked, and lo! every similitude of creeping thing, and detestable beast, and all the manufactured gods of the house of Israel,- portrayed upon the wall round about on every side;

text.NRSV: Before them stood seventy of the elders of the house of Israel, with Jaazaniah son of Shaphan standing among them. Each had his censer in his hand, and the fragrant cloud of incense was ascending.
text.Rotherham: and seventy men of the elders of the house of Israel with Jaazaniah son of Shaphan, who stood in their midst were standing before them, even every man with his censer in his hand,—and the fragrance of the cloud of incense ascending.

text.NRSV: Then he said to me, “Mortal, have you seen what the elders of the house of Israel are doing in the dark, each in his room of images? For they say, ‘The Lord does not see us, the Lord has forsaken the land.’ ”
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me, Hast thou seen Son of man what the elders of the house of Israel, are doing in the dark, every man in his image-chambers? for they are saying— Yahweh doth not see us! Yahweh hath forsaken the land!

text.NRSV: He said also to me, “You will see still greater abominations that they are committing.”
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me,— Yet again, shalt thou see great abominations, which they are committing.

text.NRSV: Then he brought me to the entrance of the north gate of the house of the Lord; women were sitting there weeping for Tammuz.
text.Rotherham: So he brought me into the opening of the gate of the house of Yahweh, which was toward the north,—and Lo! there, women sitting, weeping for Tammuz.

text.NRSV: Then he said to me, “Have you seen this, O mortal? You will see still greater abominations than these.”
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me Hast thou seen O son of man? Yet again, shalt thou see greater abominations than these.

text.NRSV: And he brought me into the inner court of the house of the Lord; there, at the entrance of the temple of the Lord, between the porch and the altar, were about twenty-five men, with their backs to the temple of the Lord, and their faces toward the east, prostrating themselves to the sun toward the east.
text.Rotherham: So he brought me into the inner court of the house of Yahweh, and lo! at the opening of the temple of Yahweh, between the porch and the altar, about twenty-five men,—their backs towards the temple of Yahweh and their faces eastward, and they were bowing down eastward unto the Sun.

text.NRSV: Then he said to me, “Have you seen this, O mortal? Is it too slight a thing for the house of Judah to commit the abominations that they are committing here, that they must fill the land with violence, and provoke me to anger over and over again? See, they are putting the branch to their nose!
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me Hast thou seen O son of man? Is it too small a thing for the house of Judah, to be committing the abominations which they have committed here,—that they have filled the land with violence and have again provoked me to anger and there they are! putting the branch to my nose.

text.NRSV: Therefore I will act in fury; my eye will not spare, nor will I have pity; and though they cry in my hearing with a loud voice, I will not listen to them.”
text.Rotherham: Therefore even I, will act with indignation, Mine eye shall not shield. Neither will I pity,— Though they have cried in mine ears with a loud voice, yet will I not hear them.

text.NRSV: Then he cried in my hearing with a loud voice, saying, “Draw near, you executioners of the city, each with his destroying weapon in his hand.”
text.Rotherham: Then cried he in mine ears with a loud voice, saying, Draw near ye that have charge of the city,-even every man with his weapon of destruction in his hand.

text.NRSV: And six men came from the direction of the upper gate, which faces north, each with his weapon for slaughter in his hand; among them was a man clothed in linen, with a writing case at his side. They went in and stood beside the bronze altar.
text.Rotherham: And lo! six men coming in out of the way of the upper gate which looketh toward the north even every man with his destructive weapon in his hand, and one man in their midst clothed with linen, having a scribe’s ink holder by his side,—so they came in and stood beside the altar of bronze.

text.NRSV: Now the glory of the God of Israel had gone up from the cherub on which it rested to the threshold of the house. The Lord called to the man clothed in linen, who had the writing case at his side,
text.Rotherham: And the glory of the God of Israel lifted itself up from off the cherub whereon it had been, unto the threshold of the house,—and he called unto the man clothed with linen, who had the scribe’s ink holder by his side.

text.NRSV: and said to him, “Go through the city, through Jerusalem, and put a mark on the foreheads of those who sigh and groan over all the abominations that are committed in it.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto him. Pass along through the midst of the city, through the midst of Jerusalem,—and set thou a mark upon the foreheads of the men who are sighing and crying over all the abominations that are being done in her midst.

text.NRSV: To the others he said in my hearing, “Pass through the city after him, and kill; your eye shall not spare, and you shall have no pity.
text.Rotherham: But unto these, said he in mine ears, Pass along through the city after him and smite,—let not your eye, shield, neither have ye pity:

text.NRSV: Cut down old men, young men and young women, little children and women; but touch no one who has the mark. And begin at my sanctuary.” So they began with the elders who were in front of the house.
text.Rotherham: elder, young man and virgin and little ones and women, shall ye slay utterly, but unto any man who hath upon him the mark, do not ye come near, and at my sanctuary, shall ye begin. So they began with the elder men. who were before the house.

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “Defile the house, and fill the courts with the slain. Go!” So they went out and killed in the city.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them Defile ye the house and fill the courts with the slain,—go ye forth! So they went forth and smote in the city.

text.NRSV: While they were killing, and I was left alone, I fell on my face and cried out, “Ah, Lord God! will you destroy all who remain of Israel as you pour out your fury upon Jerusalem?”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, while they were smiting them—I being left remaining, that I fell upon my face and made outcry, and said Alas! My Lord. Yahweh art thou about to destroy all the remnant of Israel, in that thou art pouring out of thine indignation upon Jerusalem?

text.NRSV: He said to me, “The guilt of the house of Israel and Judah is exceedingly great; the land is full of bloodshed and the city full of perversity; for they say, ‘The Lord has forsaken the land, and the Lord does not see.’
text.Rotherham: And he said unto me the iniquity of the house of Israel and Judah, is exceeding, great, and the land is filled with shed blood, and the city is full of over-reaching,—for they have said. Yahweh hath forsaken the land,— and Yahweh doth not see.

text.NRSV: As for me, my eye will not spare, nor will I have pity, but I will bring their deeds upon their heads.”
text.Rotherham: Therefore as for even me, Mine eye shall not shield. Neither will I pity,— Their way upon their own head, have I rendered.

text.NRSV: Then the man clothed in linen, with the writing case at his side, brought back word, saying, “I have done as you commanded me.”
text.Rotherham: And lo! the man clothed in linen, having the ink-holder by his side, bringing back word saying,— I have done according to all which thou didst command me.

text.NRSV: Then I looked, and on the dome over the heads of the cherubim there appeared above them something like a sapphire, in form resembling a throne.
text.Rotherham: Then looked I and lo! in the expanse which was over the head of the cherubim as a sapphire stone, as the appearance of the likeness of a throne, appeared over them.

text.NRSV: He said to the man clothed in linen, “Go within the wheelwork underneath the cherubim; fill your hands with burning coals from among the cherubim, and scatter them over the city.” He went in as I looked on.
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto the man clothed in linen—then said he Go in between the whirling wheels, even under the cherub, and fill both thy hands with live coals of fire from between the cherubim, and throw over the city. So he went in before mine eyes.

text.NRSV: Now the cherubim were standing on the south side of the house when the man went in; and a cloud filled the inner court.
text.Rotherham: Now the cherubim were standing on the right side of the house when the man went in,—and, the, cloud filled the inner court,

text.NRSV: Then the glory of the Lord rose up from the cherub to the threshold of the house; the house was filled with the cloud, and the court was full of the brightness of the glory of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then arose the glory of Yahweh from off the cherub, unto the threshold of the house,—and the house was filled with the cloud, and the, court, was filled with the brightness of the glory of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The sound of the wings of the cherubim was heard as far as the outer court, like the voice of God Almighty when he speaks.
text.Rotherham: And the sound of the wings of the cherubim was heard, as far as the outer court,—like the voice of the Almighty GOD when he speaketh,

text.NRSV: When he commanded the man clothed in linen, “Take fire from within the wheelwork, from among the cherubim,” he went in and stood beside a wheel.
text.Rotherham: So then it came to pass— when he commanded the man clothed with linen, saying, Take fire from between the whirling wheels, from between the cherubim, then went he in and stood beside the wheel.

text.NRSV: And a cherub stretched out his hand from among the cherubim to the fire that was among the cherubim, took some of it and put it into the hands of the man clothed in linen, who took it and went out.
text.Rotherham: And the cherub put forth his hand from between the cherubim unto the fire that was between the cherubim, and took up thereof and gave into the hands of him who was clothed with linen,—who took [it] and went forth,

text.NRSV: The cherubim appeared to have the form of a human hand under their wings.
text.Rotherham: Now the cherubim appeared to have. the likeness of the hand of a man, under their wings.

text.NRSV: I looked, and there were four wheels beside the cherubim, one beside each cherub; and the appearance of the wheels was like gleaming beryl.
text.Rotherham: Then looked I. and lo! four wheels by the cherubim—one wheel by one cherub, and another wheel by another cherub,—and the appearance of the wheels was to look upon like a Tarshish stone;

text.NRSV: And as for their appearance, the four had the same form, as though a wheel were within a wheel.
text.Rotherham: and as for their appearance, one likeness, had they four,-as might be a wheel in the midst of a wheel.

text.NRSV: When they moved, they moved in any of the four directions without veering as they moved; but in whatever direction the front wheel faced, the others followed without veering as they moved.
text.Rotherham: When they went, upon their four sides, they went, they turned not when they went, for to the place whithersoever the head turned, after it, they went, they turned not when they went.

text.NRSV: Their entire body, their backs, their hands, and their wings, as well as the wheels (the wheels of the four of them), were full of eyes all around.
text.Rotherham: And all their flesh, and their back, and their hands and their wings, and the wheels were full of eyes round about, even their wheels which they four had

text.NRSV: As for the wheels, they were called in my hearing “the wheelwork.”
text.Rotherham: To the wheels—to them, was made the cry— O whirling wheel! in mine ears.

text.NRSV: Each one had four faces: the first face was the face of the cherub, the second face was a human face, the third the face of a lion, and the fourth the face of an eagle.
text.Rotherham: And four faces had each one,—the first face, was the face of a cherub, and, the second face, was the face of a man and the third was the face of a lion. and the fourth, was the face of an eagle.

text.NRSV: Then the cherubim rose up. These were the living creatures that I had seen by the river Chebar.
text.Rotherham: Then arose the cherubim,—the same, was the living one, which I had seen by the river Chebar.

text.NRSV: When the cherubim moved, the wheels moved beside them; and when the cherubim lifted up their wings to rise up from the earth, the wheels at their side did not veer.
text.Rotherham: When the cherubim went, then went the wheels beside them,—and when the cherubim lifted up their wings, to arise from off the earth, the wheels themselves turned not from beside them;

text.NRSV: When they stopped, the others stopped, and when they rose up, the others rose up with them; for the spirit of the living creatures was in them.
text.Rotherham: when they stood, these stood, and when they arose, these arose with them,—for, the spirit of the living one, was in them.

text.NRSV: Then the glory of the Lord went out from the threshold of the house and stopped above the cherubim.
text.Rotherham: And when the glory of Yahweh went forth from off the threshold of the house,—and stood over the cherubim,

text.NRSV: The cherubim lifted up their wings and rose up from the earth in my sight as they went out, with the wheels beside them. They stopped at the entrance of the east gate of the house of the Lord; and the glory of the God of Israel was above them.
text.Rotherham: then the cherubim uplifted their wings and arose from the earth before mine eyes as they went forth, the wheels also in unison with them,—and it stood at the opening of the gate of the house of Yahweh that was toward the east, with the glory of the God of Israel over them, above.

text.NRSV: These were the living creatures that I saw underneath the God of Israel by the river Chebar; and I knew that they were cherubim.
text.Rotherham: The same, was the living one which I saw under the God of Israel by the river Chebar,- then knew I that cherubim, they were.

text.NRSV: Each had four faces, and each four wings, and underneath their wings something like human hands.
text.Rotherham: Four faces severally, had each one, and four wings, had each one,—and the likeness of the hands of a man, was under their wings.

text.NRSV: As for the appearance of their faces, they were the very faces whose appearance I had seen by the river Chebar. Each one moved straight ahead.
text.Rotherham: And as for the likeness of their faces, They were the faces which I saw by the river Chebar, their appearances and themselves— every one straight before him, did they go.

text.NRSV: The spirit lifted me up and brought me to the east gate of the house of the Lord, which faces east. There, at the entrance of the gateway, were twenty-five men; among them I saw Jaazaniah son of Azzur, and Pelatiah son of Benaiah, officials of the people.
text.Rotherham: And the Spirit lifted me up, and brought me into the east gate of the house of Yahweh which looketh eastward, and lo! in the opening of the gate, twenty-five men,—and I saw in their midst Jaazaniah son of Azzur and Pelatiah son of Benaiah princes of the people.

text.NRSV: He said to me, “Mortal, these are the men who devise iniquity and who give wicked counsel in this city;
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me, Son of man, These, are the men who are devising iniquity, and who are counselling wicked counsel in this city;

text.NRSV: they say, ‘The time is not near to build houses; this city is the pot, and we are the meat.’
text.Rotherham: who are saying, Not near! let us build houses,— It, is the caldron, and, we, are the flesh!

text.NRSV: Therefore prophesy against them; prophesy, O mortal.”
text.Rotherham: Therefore, prophesy against them, —prophesy. Son of man.

text.NRSV: Then the spirit of the Lord fell upon me, and he said to me, “Say, Thus says the Lord: This is what you think, O house of Israel; I know the things that come into your mind.
text.Rotherham: Then fell upon me the Spirit of Yahweh, and he said unto me Say—Thus, saith Yahweh, Thus have ye said O house of Israel, Yea, the things that come up on your spirit, I, know every one.

text.NRSV: You have killed many in this city, and have filled its streets with the slain.
text.Rotherham: Ye have multiplied your slain in this city,- And have filled her streets with slain.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God: The slain whom you have placed within it are the meat, and this city is the pot; but you shall be taken out of it.
text.Rotherham: Therefore Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Your slain. whom ye have laid in her midst, They are the flesh, and She is the caldron,—when ye are taken out of her midst.

text.NRSV: You have feared the sword; and I will bring the sword upon you, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: A sword, have ye feared,— And a sword, will I bring in upon you, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.

text.NRSV: I will take you out of it and give you over to the hands of foreigners, and execute judgments upon you.
text.Rotherham: So will I take you forth out of her midst, and will deliver you into the hand of foreigners, —and will execute upon you judgments.

text.NRSV: You shall fall by the sword; I will judge you at the border of Israel. And you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: By the sword, shall ye fall, Upon the boundary of Israel, will I judge you, —So shall ye know that, I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: This city shall not be your pot, and you shall not be the meat inside it; I will judge you at the border of Israel.
text.Rotherham: This city shall not serve you for a caldron, Neither shall, ye, serve in the midst thereof for flesh,—Near the boundary of Israel, will I judge you,

text.NRSV: Then you shall know that I am the Lord, whose statutes you have not followed, and whose ordinances you have not kept, but you have acted according to the ordinances of the nations that are around you."
text.Rotherham: So shall ye know that, I, am Yahweh. In whose statutes, ye have not walked And whose appointments, ye have not executed,— But according to the appointments of the nations that are round about you, have ye done.

text.NRSV: Now while I was prophesying, Pelatiah son of Benaiah died. Then I fell down on my face, cried with a loud voice, and said, “Ah, Lord God! will you make a full end of the remnant of Israel?”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass as I prophesied, that Pelatiah son of Benaiah died,—so then I fell down upon my face and made outcry with a loud voice and said— Alas! My Lord Yahweh! A full end, art thou making of the remnant of Israel?

text.NRSV: Then the word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, your kinsfolk, your own kin, your fellow exiles, the whole house of Israel, all of them, are those of whom the inhabitants of Jerusalem have said, “They have gone far from the Lord; to us this land is given for a possession.”
text.Rotherham: Son of man Thine own brethren, thine own brethren the men of thy kindred, even all the house of Israel all of it are they to whom the inhabitants of Jerusalem have said, Get you far away from Yahweh, To us, it is that the land, hath been given, for a possession!

text.NRSV: Therefore say: Thus says the Lord God: Though I removed them far away among the nations, and though I scattered them among the countries, yet I have been a sanctuary to them for a little while in the countries where they have gone.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, say, Thus saith My Lord. Yahweh, Although I have removed them far away among the nations, And although I have dispersed them throughout the lands Yet have I become to them a sanctuary for a little while, in the lands which they have entered

text.NRSV: Therefore say: Thus says the Lord God: I will gather you from the peoples, and assemble you out of the countries where you have been scattered, and I will give you the land of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Therefore say, Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, Therefore will I assemble you from among the peoples, And gather you out of the lands wherein ye have been dispersed,- And will give you the soil of Israel.

text.NRSV: When they come there, they will remove from it all its detestable things and all its abominations.
text.Rotherham: So shall they come in thither,— and take away all her detestable things, and all her abominations, out of her;

text.NRSV: I will give them one heart, and put a new spirit within them; I will remove the heart of stone from their flesh and give them a heart of flesh,
text.Rotherham: And I will give them another heart, And a new spirit, will I put within you,—And will take away the heart of stone out of their flesh, and give them a heart of flesh:

text.NRSV: so that they may follow my statutes and keep my ordinances and obey them. Then they shall be my people, and I will be their God.
text.Rotherham: To the end that in my statutes, they may walk, And mine appointments, they may keep, and do them,— So shall they become my people, And I will become their God.

text.NRSV: But as for those whose heart goes after their detestable things and their abominations, I will bring their deeds upon their own heads, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: But as for them who having a heart for their detestable things and for their abominations after their own heart, do walk, Their way upon their own heads, will I render, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Then the cherubim lifted up their wings, with the wheels beside them; and the glory of the God of Israel was above them.
text.Rotherham: Then did the cherubim lift up their wings, the wheels also going in unison with them,— the glory of the God of Israel, being over them above.

text.NRSV: And the glory of the Lord ascended from the middle of the city, and stopped on the mountain east of the city.
text.Rotherham: Thus went up the glory of Yahweh, out from the midst of the city,—and stood on the mountain, which is on the east of the city.

text.NRSV: The spirit lifted me up and brought me in a vision by the spirit of God into Chaldea, to the exiles. Then the vision that I had seen left me.
text.Rotherham: And, the spirit lifted me up, and brought me to Chaldea unto them of the captivity, in the vision by the Spirit of God,—thus went up from me, the vision which I had seen.

text.NRSV: And I told the exiles all the things that the Lord had shown me.
text.Rotherham: So I spake unto them of the captivity,—all the words of Yahweh which he had shewed me.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, you are living in the midst of a rebellious house, who have eyes to see but do not see, who have ears to hear but do not hear;
text.Rotherham: Son of man. In the midst of a perverse house, dost thou dwell,— Who have eyes to see—and have not seen. Ears have they to hear—and have no heard, For a perverse house, they are.

text.NRSV: for they are a rebellious house. Therefore, mortal, prepare for yourself an exile’s baggage, and go into exile by day in their sight; you shall go like an exile from your place to another place in their sight. Perhaps they will understand, though they are a rebellious house.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore Son of man, Prepare thee baggage for exile, and exile thyself, by day, before their eyes,—so shalt thou exile thyself out of thy place unto another place before their eyes, peradventure they will consider— though a perverse house, they are.

text.NRSV: You shall bring out your baggage by day in their sight, as baggage for exile; and you shall go out yourself at evening in their sight, as those do who go into exile.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shalt thou take forth thy baggage as baggage for exile, by day before their eyes,—and thou thyself, shalt go forth in the evening, before their eyes, like them who go forth to exile.

text.NRSV: Dig through the wall in their sight, and carry the baggage out through it.
text.Rotherham: Before their eyes, break thou forth by thyself through the wall,—and carry forth through it.

text.NRSV: In their sight you shall lift the baggage upon your shoulder, and carry it out in the dark; you shall cover your face, so that you may not see the land; for I have made you a sign for the house of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Before their eyes, Upon the shoulder, shalt thou lift it. In thick darkness, take it forth, Thy face, shalt thou cover so that thou see not the land; For a sign, have I appointed thee to the house of Israel.

text.NRSV: I did just as I was commanded. I brought out my baggage by day, as baggage for exile, and in the evening I dug through the wall with my own hands; I brought it out in the dark, carrying it on my shoulder in their sight.
text.Rotherham: And I did so, just as I was commanded, My baggage, took I forth as baggage for exile by day, and in the evening, I brake forth by myself through the wall, by force: in the twilight, I took it forth—on to my shoulder, I lifted it, before their eyes.

text.NRSV: In the morning the word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me in the morning, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, has not the house of Israel, the rebellious house, said to you, “What are you doing?”
text.Rotherham: Son of man, Have not the house of Israel the perverse house, said unto thee,— What art thou doing?

text.NRSV: Say to them, “Thus says the Lord God: This oracle concerns the prince in Jerusalem and all the house of Israel in it.”
text.Rotherham: Say unto them, Thus, saith My Lord, Yahweh,— [For] the Bearer, is this burden, in Jerusalem, and [for] all the house of Israel such as are in their midst.

text.NRSV: Say, “I am a sign for you: as I have done, so shall it be done to them; they shall go into exile, into captivity.”
text.Rotherham: Say, I, am your sign: As I have done, so. shall it be done to them, Into exile—into captivity, shall they go.

text.NRSV: And the prince who is among them shall lift his baggage on his shoulder in the dark, and shall go out; he shall dig through the wall to carry it out through it; he shall cover his face, so that he may not see the land with his eyes.
text.Rotherham: Yea, the Bearer who is in their midst, Upon his shoulder, shall lift it. In thick darkness, shall he go forth, Through the wall, shall they break to bear forth through it,— His face, shall he cover, to the end that, his own eye, may not see the land.

text.NRSV: I will spread my net over him, and he shall be caught in my snare; and I will bring him to Babylon, the land of the Chaldeans, yet he shall not see it; and he shall die there.
text.Rotherham: Then will I spread my net over him, and he shall be taken in my snare; And I will take him to Babylon. in the land of the Chaldeans, The which, indeed, he shall not see. And yet there, shall he die!

text.NRSV: I will scatter to every wind all who are around him, his helpers and all his troops; and I will unsheathe the sword after them.
text.Rotherham: And all who are round about him to help him and all his troops, will I scatter to every wind,—and, a sword, will I make bare after them.

text.NRSV: And they shall know that I am the Lord, when I disperse them among the nations and scatter them through the countries.
text.Rotherham: So shall they know that, I, am Yahweh,— By my dispersing them among the nations, And scattering them throughout the lands.

text.NRSV: But I will let a few of them escape from the sword, from famine and pestilence, so that they may tell of all their abominations among the nations where they go; then they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Yet will I leave remaining of them men easily counted, from the sword from the famine and from the pestilence,—that they may recount all their abominations among the nations whither they have come, So shall they know that, I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, eat your bread with quaking, and drink your water with trembling and with fearfulness;
