text.NRSV: and I will bring you into the wilderness of the peoples, and there I will enter into judgment with you face to face.
text.Rotherham: And I will bring you into the desert of the peoples,—and will contend with you there, face to face.

text.NRSV: As I entered into judgment with your ancestors in the wilderness of the land of Egypt, so I will enter into judgment with you, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: Just as I contended with your fathers in the desert of the land of Egypt, So will I contend with you, Declareth My Lord Yahweh;

text.NRSV: I will make you pass under the staff, and will bring you within the bond of the covenant.
text.Rotherham: And I will cause you to pass under the rod And will bring you into the bond of the covenant;

text.NRSV: I will purge out the rebels among you, and those who transgress against me; I will bring them out of the land where they reside as aliens, but they shall not enter the land of Israel. Then you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And will sever from you those who are rebelling and those who are transgressing against me, Out of the land of their sojournings, will I bring them forth, Yet upon the soil of Israel, shall they not enter, So shall ye know that, I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: As for you, O house of Israel, thus says the Lord God: Go serve your idols, every one of you now and hereafter, if you will not listen to me; but my holy name you shall no more profane with your gifts and your idols.
text.Rotherham: But as for you, O house of Israel Thus, saith my Lord. Yahweh, Go. serve every man his own manufactured gods, Yet afterwards, surely Ye shall be ready to hearken unto me,— And my holy name, shall ye profane no more, With your gifts, and With your manufactured gods.

text.NRSV: For on my holy mountain, the mountain height of Israel, says the Lord God, there all the house of Israel, all of them, shall serve me in the land; there I will accept them, and there I will require your contributions and the choicest of your gifts, with all your sacred offerings.
text.Rotherham: For in my holy mountain. In the mountain of the height of Israel, Declareth, My Lord Yahweh, There shall all the house of Israel serve me, all of it, in the land, There will I accept them, and There, will I seek your heave-offerings and the firstfruits of your gifts, in all your holy things:

text.NRSV: As a pleasing odor I will accept you, when I bring you out from the peoples, and gather you out of the countries where you have been scattered; and I will manifest my holiness among you in the sight of the nations.
text.Rotherham: As a satisfying odour, will I accept you, Through my bringing you forth from among the peoples, And gathering you out of the lands, throughout which ye have been dispersed; Thus will I hallow myself in you before the eyes of the nations.

text.NRSV: You shall know that I am the Lord, when I bring you into the land of Israel, the country that I swore to give to your ancestors.
text.Rotherham: So shall ye know that I am Yahweh, Through my bringing you in unto the soil of Israel, Unto the land as to which I lifted up my hand, to give it unto your fathers.

text.NRSV: There you shall remember your ways and all the deeds by which you have polluted yourselves; and you shall loathe yourselves for all the evils that you have committed.
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye call to mind. there, your own ways and all your own doings, wherewith ye had defiled yourselves,- And ye shall become loathsome in your own sight, for all your wickednesses, which ye have done.

text.NRSV: And you shall know that I am the Lord, when I deal with you for my name’s sake, not according to your evil ways, or corrupt deeds, O house of Israel, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: So shall ye know that, I, am Yahweh, Through my dealing effectively with you, for the sake of mine own Name,- Not according to your own wicked ways. Nor according to your corrupted doings. O house of Israel, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me. saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, set your face toward the south, preach against the south, and prophesy against the forest land in the Negeb;
text.Rotherham: Son of man Set thy face the way towards Teman, And drop thy word against the land of the sun, And prophesy unto the forest of the field of the South;

text.NRSV: say to the forest of the Negeb, Hear the word of the Lord: Thus says the Lord God, I will kindle a fire in you, and it shall devour every green tree in you and every dry tree; the blazing flame shall not be quenched, and all faces from south to north shall be scorched by it.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt say to the forest of the South Hear the word of Yahweh,— Thus saith My Lord Yahweh-Behold me! kindling in thee a fire. And it shall devour in thee every moist tree and every dry tree. The spreading flame shall not be quenched, Then shall be scorched thereby all faces from south to north;

text.NRSV: All flesh shall see that I the Lord have kindled it; it shall not be quenched.
text.Rotherham: And all flesh shall see that I. Yahweh have kindled it,- It shall not be quenched.

text.NRSV: Then I said, “Ah, Lord God! they are saying of me, ‘Is he not a maker of allegories?’ ”
text.Rotherham: Then said I, Ah! My Lord Yahweh: They are saying of me, Is not he a putter forth of parables?

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, set your face toward Jerusalem and preach against the sanctuaries; prophesy against the land of Israel
text.Rotherham: Son of man Set thy face against Jerusalem, And drop thy word against the holy places And prophesy against the soil of Israel.

text.NRSV: and say to the land of Israel, Thus says the Lord: I am coming against you, and will draw my sword out of its sheath, and will cut off from you both righteous and wicked.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt say then to the soil of Israel Thus saith Yahweh, Behold me against thee, Therefore will I bring forth my sword out of its sheath,—and will cut off from thee the righteous and the lawless.

text.NRSV: Because I will cut off from you both righteous and wicked, therefore my sword shall go out of its sheath against all flesh from south to north;
text.Rotherham: Because I have cut off from thee the righteous and the lawless, Therefore shall my sword go forth out of its sheath against all flesh from south to north:

text.NRSV: and all flesh shall know that I the Lord have drawn my sword out of its sheath; it shall not be sheathed again.
text.Rotherham: So shall all flesh know that, I, Yahweh have brought forth my sword out of its sheath,— it shall not return any more.

text.NRSV: Moan therefore, mortal; moan with breaking heart and bitter grief before their eyes.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore O son of man sigh,— In the sharp pain of thy loins and in bitterness, shalt thou sigh before their eyes.

text.NRSV: And when they say to you, “Why do you moan?” you shall say, “Because of the news that has come. Every heart will melt and all hands will be feeble, every spirit will faint and all knees will turn to water. See, it comes and it will be fulfilled,” says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass when they shall say unto thee, Wherefore art thou sighing? that thou shalt say, For the report because it cometh, When every heart shall melt. And all hands be unnerved And every spirit shall become faint. And all knees shall be weak as water, Lo! it cometh and shall be brought to pass, Saith My Lord, Yahweh.

text.NRSV: And the word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me. saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, prophesy and say: Thus says the Lord; Say: A sword, a sword is sharpened, and also polished;
text.Rotherham: Son of man, Prophesy and thou shalt say, Thus saith My Lord,— Say, A sword! a sword! Sharpened moreover also furbished:

text.NRSV: it is sharpened for slaughter, polished to flash like lightning! How can we make merry? You have despised the rod, and every stick.
text.Rotherham: To make a slaughter, is it sharpened, That it may flash as lightning, is it furbished. Or shall we flourish the royal rod of my son which despiseth every tree?

text.NRSV: The sword is given to be polished, to be grasped; it is sharpened, it is polished, to be placed in the slayer’s hand.
text.Rotherham: It hath. however been given to be furbished that it may be grasped by the hand:The same is a sword sharpened. Yea the same, is furbished, Ready to be given into the hand of the slayer.

text.NRSV: Cry and wail, mortal, for it is against my people; it is against all the princes of Israel; they are delivered over to the sword with my people. Therefore strike your thigh!
text.Rotherham: Make an outcry and howl son of man, For the same hath come against my people, the same is against all the princes of Israel,- Who are thrown to the sword with my people, Therefore smite thou upon thy thigh.

text.NRSV: For consider: What if you despise the rod? Shall it not happen? says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: For trial hath been made, What then, even though the royal rod despiseth, shall it not be? Demandeth My Lord. Yahweh.

text.NRSV: You therefore, mortal, prophesy; strike hand to hand. Let the sword fall twice, let it fall thrice—it is a sword for slaughter, a sword for great slaughter—it surrounds them;
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, son of man, Prophesy, and smite thy hands together, And let the sword smite twice thrice the sword of the slain! the same is the great sword of the slain, that which hemmeth them in.

text.NRSV: therefore hearts melt and many stumble. At all their gates I have set the point of the sword. Ah! it is made for flashing, it is polished for slaughter.
text.Rotherham: That the heart may melt And the overthrow be multiplied at all their gates, I have recompensed a slaying by the sword: Surely it hath been made ready to flash like lightning, keen for slaughter.

text.NRSV: Attack to the right! Push to the left! Wherever your edge is directed.
text.Rotherham: One firm stroke to the right. Turn to the left,- Whithersoever thine edge is directed,

text.NRSV: I too will strike hand to hand, and I will satisfy my fury; I the Lord have spoken.
text.Rotherham: Yea even I myself, will smite my hands together, And will cause mine indignation to find rest,— I Yahweh, have spoken.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, mark out two roads for the sword of the king of Babylon to come; both of them shall issue from the same land. And make a signpost, make it for a fork in the road leading to a city;
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore son of man. Appoint thee two ways For the sword of the king of Babylon to enter, Out of one land shall those two ways come,— And a hand, engrave thou, At the head of the way to the city, engrave it.;

text.NRSV: mark out the road for the sword to come to Rabbah of the Ammonites or to Judah and to Jerusalem the fortified.
text.Rotherham: A way, shalt thou appoint, for the sword to enter. Even to Rabbah of the sons of Ammon,— And to Judah against Jerusalem, the defenced,

text.NRSV: For the king of Babylon stands at the parting of the way, at the fork in the two roads, to use divination; he shakes the arrows, he consults the teraphim, he inspects the liver.
text.Rotherham: For the king of Babylon, hath come to a stand, At the parting of the way At the head of the two ways To divine a divination:He hath shaken with arrows He hath asked of the household gods, He hath inspected the liver.

text.NRSV: Into his right hand comes the lot for Jerusalem, to set battering rams, to call out for slaughter, for raising the battle cry, to set battering rams against the gates, to cast up a mound, to build a siege-wall.
text.Rotherham: On his right hand, hath come the divination—Jerusalem! To plant battering-rams To open a hole by breach To lift up the voice with a war-shout,— To plant battering-rams against the gates, To cast up an earth-work To build a siege-wall.

text.NRSV: It will seem to them like a false divination; they have sworn solemn oaths; but he brings their guilt to mind, in order to capture them.
text.Rotherham: Though it will become to them a veritably false divination in their eyes, even when bound by oaths to them Yet, he, calling to mind iniquity, [will determine] that it shall be captured.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God: Because you have brought your guilt to mind, in that your transgressions are uncovered, so that in all your deeds your sins appear—because you have come to mind, you shall be taken in hand.
text.Rotherham: Therefore Thus saith My Lord. Yahweh, Because ye have brought to mind your iniquity, by revealing your transgressions causing your sins to appear in all your doings,- Because ye have brought yourselves to mind By force shall ye be captured.

text.NRSV: As for you, vile, wicked prince of Israel, whose day has come, the time of final punishment,
text.Rotherham: Thou therefore, O profane, lawless one, prince of Israel,- Whose day hath come, in a time of final iniquity:

text.NRSV: thus says the Lord God: Remove the turban and take off the crown; things shall not remain as they are. Exalt that which is low, and abase that which is high.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith My Lord. Yahweh, Remove the turban, And lift off the crown,— This not that, The abased, exalt, And the exalted, abase.

text.NRSV: A ruin, a ruin, a ruin, I will make it! (Such as has never occurred, until he comes whose right it is; to him I will give it.)
text.Rotherham: An overthrow overthrow overthrow, will I make it,— Even this, hath not befallen until the coming of One to whom belongeth the right Then will I bestow it.

text.NRSV: As for you, mortal, prophesy, and say, Thus says the Lord God concerning the Ammonites, and concerning their reproach; say: A sword, a sword! Drawn for slaughter, polished to consume, to flash like lightning.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, son of man Prophesy and say, thus, saith My Lord. Yahweh, As to the sons of Ammon and as to their reproach Yea thou shalt say. A sword! a sword! Drawn for slaughter, Furbished, that when it beginneth, it may flash like lightning;

text.NRSV: Offering for you false visions, divining lies for you, they place you over the necks of the vile, wicked ones whose day has come, the time of final punishment.
text.Rotherham: Since they see for thee, false visions, Since they divine for thee lies, To stretch thee upon the necks of them who are the most profaned of lawless ones, Whose day hath come, in a time of final iniquity,

text.NRSV: Return it to its sheath! In the place where you were created, in the land of your origin, I will judge you.
text.Rotherham: Put it back into its sheath! In the place where thou wast created In the land of thy nativity, will I judge thee

text.NRSV: I will pour out my indignation upon you, with the fire of my wrath I will blow upon you; I will deliver you into the hands of brutal people, skilled in destruction.
text.Rotherham: Then will I pour out upon thee mine indignation, With the fire of mine outburst, will I blow upon thee; and will deliver thee up into the hand of brutish men, skilled to destroy.

text.NRSV: You shall be fuel for the fire, your blood shall be in the midst of the earth; you shall be remembered no more; for I the Lord have spoken.
text.Rotherham: For the fire, shalt thou serve as fuel, Thy blood shall be in the midst of the land,- Thou shalt not be remembered, For I, Yahweh, have spoken.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying:

text.NRSV: You, mortal, will you judge, will you judge the bloody city? Then declare to her all her abominations.
text.Rotherham: Thou therefore son of man, Wilt thou judge wilt thou judge the city of bloodshed; and cause her to know all her abominations?

text.NRSV: Say, Thus says the Lord God: A city! Shedding blood within her; her time has come; making idols, defiling herself.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt say then Thus saith My Lord. Yahweh, A city shedding blood in her own midst That her time might come, Therefore hath she been making manufactured gods for herself, to be defiled.

text.NRSV: You have become guilty by the blood that you have shed, and defiled by the idols that you have made; you have brought your day near, the appointed time of your years has come. Therefore I have made you a disgrace to the nations, and a mockery to all the countries.
text.Rotherham: Because of the blood which thou hast shed, hast thou become guilty. And with the manufactured gods which thou hast made, hast thou defiled thyself, Thus hast thou brought near thy days, And hast come unto thy years,— For this cause, have I delivered thee As a reproach to the nations, And as a derision to all the lands.

text.NRSV: Those who are near and those who are far from you will mock you, you infamous one, full of tumult.
text.Rotherham: They who are near and they who are far off from thee shall shew themselves derisive over thee,— O thou of impure name, abounding in confusion!

text.NRSV: See! The princes of Israel in you, everyone according to his power, have been bent on shedding blood.
text.Rotherham: Lo! the princes of Israel, every man for his own arm, have they been in thee, For the sake of shedding blood.

text.NRSV: Father and mother are treated with contempt in you; the alien resides within you who suffers extortion; the orphan and the widow are wronged in you.
text.Rotherham: Father and mother, have they despised in thee, To the sojourner, have they dealt with oppression in the midst of thee,— The fatherless and the widow, have they maltreated in thee:

text.NRSV: You have despised my holy things, and profaned my sabbaths.
text.Rotherham: My holy things, hast thou despised,- And my sabbaths, hast thou profaned:

text.NRSV: In you are those who slander in order to shed blood; in you are those who eat upon the mountains; in you are those who commit lewdness.
text.Rotherham: Slanders, have arisen in thee For the sake of shedding blood,- And unto the mountains, have they eaten in thee, Lewdness, have they practised in the midst of thee:

text.NRSV: In you men uncover their fathers’ nakedness; in you they violate women in their menstrual uncleanness.
text.Rotherham: A father’s shame hath been uncovered in thee, Her that was unclean in her removal, have they humbled in thee;

text.NRSV: One commits abomination with his neighbor’s wife; another lewdly defiles his daughter-in-law; another in you violates his sister, his father’s daughter.
text.Rotherham: And, one man, with the wife of his neighbour, hath wrought abomination, And another, hath defiled, his daughter-in-law, by shameful deed,— And another, his own sister daughter of his own father, hath humbled in thee:

text.NRSV: In you they take bribes to shed blood; you take both interest and profit, and make gain of your neighbors by extortion; and you have forgotten me, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: Bribes, have they accepted in thee, For the sake of shedding blood,-Interest and increase, hast thou accepted And thou hast overreached thy neighbour by oppression, And me, hast thou forgotten, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.

text.NRSV: See now, I strike my hands together at the dishonest gain you have made, and at the blood that has been shed within you.
text.Rotherham: Lo! therefore I have smitten my hand, At thine unjust gain which thou hast made— And over thy bloodshed, which they have caused in thy midst.

text.NRSV: Can your courage endure, or can your hands remain strong in the days that I shall deal with you? I the Lord have spoken, and I will do it.
text.Rotherham: Can thy heart endure or can thy hands be strong in the days when I am dealing with thee? I. Yahweh have spoken and will perform.

text.NRSV: I will scatter you among the nations and disperse you through the countries, and I will purge your uncleanness out of you.
text.Rotherham: Therefore will I disperse thee among the nations, And scatter thee throughout the lands,— And will cause to cease thine impurity out of thee;

text.NRSV: And I shall be profaned through you in the sight of the nations; and you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So shalt thou be profaned in thyself, before the eyes of nations, Thus shalt thou know that, I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, the house of Israel has become dross to me; all of them, silver and bronze and tin and iron and lead in the smelter, have become dross.
text.Rotherham: Son of man, The house of Israel hath become to me, dross,— They all are copper and tin and iron and lead in the midst of a furnace, The dross of silver, have they become.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God: Because you have all become dross, I will gather you into the midst of Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Because ye all are become dross, Therefore, behold me! gathering you into the midst of Jerusalem:

text.NRSV: As one gathers silver, bronze, iron, lead, and tin into a smelter, to blow the fire upon them in order to melt them; so I will gather you in my anger and in my wrath, and I will place you there and melt you.
text.Rotherham: As they gather silver and copper and iron and lead and tin into the midst of a furnace, to blow upon it the fire to melt it, So, will I gather in mine anger and in mine indignation, and will let be and will melt you;

text.NRSV: I will gather you and blow upon you with the fire of my wrath, and you shall be melted within it.
text.Rotherham: Yea I will gather you together, and blow upon you, with the fire of my wrath,— And ye shall be melted in the midst thereof:

text.NRSV: As silver is melted in a smelter, so you shall be melted in it; and you shall know that I the Lord have poured out my wrath upon you.
text.Rotherham: As silver is melted in he midst of a furnace, So, shall ye be melted in the midst thereof,— Then shall ye know that, I Yahweh, have poured out mine indignation upon you.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, say to her: You are a land that has not been cleansed, not rained upon in the day of indignation.
text.Rotherham: Son of man, say to her. Thou, art a land, that is not to be rained upon, nor to receive fruitful showers, in the day of indignation.

text.NRSV: Its princes within it are like a roaring lion tearing the prey; they have devoured human lives; they have taken treasure and precious things; they have made many widows within it.
text.Rotherham: Because her princes in her midst are like a roaring lion rending prey,— Life, have they devoured Wealth and precious things, have they been wont to take, Her widows, have they multiplied in her midst.

text.NRSV: Its priests have done violence to my law and have profaned my holy things; they have made no distinction between the holy and the common, neither have they taught the difference between the unclean and the clean, and they have disregarded my sabbaths, so that I am profaned among them.
text.Rotherham: Her priests have done violence to my law And have profaned my holy things, Between the hallowed and the common, have they put no difference, And between the unclean and clean, have they not taught men to discern,- And from my sabbaths, have they hid their eyes, So that I have been profaned in their midst.

text.NRSV: Its officials within it are like wolves tearing the prey, shedding blood, destroying lives to get dishonest gain.
text.Rotherham: Her rulers within her have been like wolves, rending prey,- In shedding blood In destroying lives, For the sake of getting dishonest gain.

text.NRSV: Its prophets have smeared whitewash on their behalf, seeing false visions and divining lies for them, saying, “Thus says the Lord God,” when the Lord has not spoken.
text.Rotherham: And her prophets have coated it for them with whitewash, Seeing visions of falsehood And divining for them lies, Saying, Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, when Yahweh, hath not spoken.

text.NRSV: The people of the land have practiced extortion and committed robbery; they have oppressed the poor and needy, and have extorted from the alien without justice.
text.Rotherham: The people of the land have exacted oppression, and seized plunder,- Even the afflicted and the needy, have they maltreated, And the sojourner have they oppressed without justice.

text.NRSV: And I sought for anyone among them who would repair the wall and stand in the breach before me on behalf of the land, so that I would not destroy it; but I found no one.
text.Rotherham: Then sought I from among them, a man Who could build up a wall And stand in the breach before me in behalf of the land So that I might not destroy her,— But I found none.

text.NRSV: Therefore I have poured out my indignation upon them; I have consumed them with the fire of my wrath; I have returned their conduct upon their heads, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: Therefore have I poured out upon them mine indignation, In the fire of mine outburst, have I consumed them,— Their way, upon their own head, have I placed Declareth, My Lord Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me saying,

text.NRSV: Mortal, there were two women, the daughters of one mother;
text.Rotherham: Son of man, Two women, daughters of one mother, there were;

text.NRSV: they played the whore in Egypt; they played the whore in their youth; there their breasts were pressed and their virgin bosoms handled.
text.Rotherham: And they became unchaste in Egypt, In their youth, became they unchaste, There, were handled their breasts, And there, were pressed their virgin bosoms.

text.NRSV: Oholah was the name of the elder and Oholibah the name of her sister. They became mine, and they bore sons and daughters. As for their names, Oholah is Samaria, and Oholibah is Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And their names were Oholah the elder And Oholibah—her sister, And they became mine and bare sons and daughters,- And as for their names, Samaria was Oholah, And Jerusalem, was Oholibah.

text.NRSV: Oholah played the whore while she was mine; she lusted after her lovers the Assyrians, warriors
text.Rotherham: Then became Oholah unchaste after she had become mine- And lusted after her lovers, For Assyrians, so warlike,

text.NRSV: clothed in blue, governors and deputies, all of them handsome young men, horsemen riding on horses.
text.Rotherham: Clothed in blue Governors and deputies, Attractive young men all of them,-Horsemen riding on horses.

text.NRSV: She bestowed her whorings on them, the choicest men of Assyria all of them; and she defiled herself with all the idols of everyone for whom she lusted.
text.Rotherham: So she bestowed her unchastities upon them, The choicest men of Assyria all of them,- And, with whomsoever she lusted after-with all their manufactured gods, she defiled herself.

text.NRSV: She did not give up her whorings that she had practiced since Egypt; for in her youth men had lain with her and handled her virgin bosom and poured out their lust upon her.
text.Rotherham: And, her unchaste doings from the time she was in Egypt, had she not forsaken, For with her, had they lain in her youth, Yea they had pressed her virgin bosoms,- and had poured out their unchastity upon her.

text.NRSV: Therefore I delivered her into the hands of her lovers, into the hands of the Assyrians, for whom she lusted.
text.Rotherham: therefore, did I deliver her into the hand of her lovers,- into the hand of the sons of Assyria, after whom she lusted:

text.NRSV: These uncovered her nakedness; they seized her sons and her daughters; and they killed her with the sword. Judgment was executed upon her, and she became a byword among women.
text.Rotherham: They disclosed her shame, Her sons and her daughters:, took they away, And herself—with the sword, they slew,— Thus became she a by-word among women, when judgments, they had executed upon her.

text.NRSV: Her sister Oholibah saw this, yet she was more corrupt than she in her lusting and in her whorings, which were worse than those of her sister.
text.Rotherham: And though her sister Oholibah saw, Yet became she more corrupt in her lust than the other,- And her unchaste doings exceeded the unchaste doings of her sister.

text.NRSV: She lusted after the Assyrians, governors and deputies, warriors clothed in full armor, horsemen riding on horses, all of them handsome young men.
text.Rotherham: After the sons of Assyria:, she lusted Governors and deputies so warlike. Clothed in splendid array, Horsemen, riding on horses, Attractive young men, all of them.

text.NRSV: And I saw that she was defiled; they both took the same way.
text.Rotherham: So I saw that she defiled herself,— One way, had they both.

text.NRSV: But she carried her whorings further; she saw male figures carved on the wall, images of the Chaldeans portrayed in vermilion,
text.Rotherham: Yet did she add, unto her unchaste doings, when she saw men portrayed upon the wall, likenesses of Chaldeans, Portrayed with vermilion:

text.NRSV: with belts around their waists, with flowing turbans on their heads, all of them looking like officers—a picture of Babylonians whose native land was Chaldea.
text.Rotherham: Girded with waistcloths upon their loins With overhanging high turbans upon their heads, In appearance, knights all of them,-The likeness of the sons of Babylon of Chaldea, the land of their birth.

text.NRSV: When she saw them she lusted after them, and sent messengers to them in Chaldea.
text.Rotherham: Then lusted she after them, as soon as her eyes beheld them,— And she sent messengers unto them to Chaldea.

text.NRSV: And the Babylonians came to her into the bed of love, and they defiled her with their lust; and after she had been defiled by them, she turned from them in disgust.
text.Rotherham: Then came in unto her the sons of Babylon into the bed of endearments, and defiled her with their unchaste doings,- And she defiled herself with them, And then was her soul torn from them.

text.NRSV: When she carried on her whorings so openly and flaunted her nakedness, I turned in disgust from her, as I had turned from her sister.
text.Rotherham: Thus disclosed she her unchaste desires, And disclosed her shame,- So my soul was torn from her, Just as my soul had been torn from her sister.

text.NRSV: Yet she increased her whorings, remembering the days of her youth, when she played the whore in the land of Egypt
text.Rotherham: Yea she multiplied her unchaste desires,-calling to mind the days of her youth, when she was unchaste in the land of Egypt; -

text.NRSV: and lusted after her paramours there, whose members were like those of donkeys, and whose emission was like that of stallions.
text.Rotherham: So she lusted after their courtiers,- Whose flesh was the flesh of asses, and Whose issue was the issue of horses.

text.NRSV: Thus you longed for the lewdness of your youth, when the Egyptians handled your bosom and pressed your young breasts.
text.Rotherham: Yea thou didst look about for the lewdness of thy youth,- When were pressed by the Egyptians. thy bosoms, When they handled thy youthful breasts.

text.NRSV: Therefore, O Oholibah, thus says the Lord God: I will rouse against you your lovers from whom you turned in disgust, and I will bring them against you from every side:
text.Rotherham: Therefore, O Oholibah, Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, Behold me! rousing up thy lovers against thee, Even them from whom thy soul hath been torn,- And I will bring them against thee from every side:

text.NRSV: the Babylonians and all the Chaldeans, Pekod and Shoa and Koa, and all the Assyrians with them, handsome young men, governors and deputies all of them, officers and warriors, all of them riding on horses.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Babylon and all Chaldeans Pekod and Shoa and Koa, All the sons of Assyria with them,— Attractive young men Governors and deputies, all of them. Knights and men of renown, Riders on horses all of them;

text.NRSV: They shall come against you from the north with chariots and wagons and a host of peoples; they shall set themselves against you on every side with buckler, shield, and helmet, and I will commit the judgment to them, and they shall judge you according to their ordinances.
text.Rotherham: And they shall come upon thee— With battle-axe, chariot and wheel And with a gathered host of peoples, :Shield and buckler and helmet, will they array against thee on every side,- And I will put before them judgment, And they shall judge thee with their judgments;

text.NRSV: I will direct my jealousy against you, and they shall deal with you in fury; they shall cut off your nose and your ears, and your survivors shall fall by the sword. They shall seize your sons and your daughters, and your survivors shall be devoured by fire.
text.Rotherham: When I set forth my jealousy against thee, Then will they deal with thee in indignation, :Thy nose and thine ears, will they remove, And that which is left of thee, by the sword, shall fall,— They, will take away thy sons and thy daughters, And that which is left of thee, shall be devoured by the fire;

text.NRSV: They shall also strip you of your clothes and take away your beautiful jewels.
text.Rotherham: And they shall strip off thy raiment,- And take away thine adorning jewels!

text.NRSV: Thus I will put an end to your lewdness and your whoring brought from the land of Egypt; you shall not lift up your eyes to them, or remember Egypt any more.
text.Rotherham: Thus will I cause thy lewdness to cease, from thee Even thine unchastity brought from the land of Egypt,- So that thou shalt not lift up thine eyes unto them, And Egypt, shalt thou not call to mind any more.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord God: I will deliver you into the hands of those whom you hate, into the hands of those from whom you turned in disgust;
text.Rotherham: For thus saith My Lord, Yahweh, Behold me! delivering thee up, into the hand of them whom thou hatest, Into the hand of them from whom thy soul hath been torn;

text.NRSV: and they shall deal with you in hatred, and take away all the fruit of your labor, and leave you naked and bare, and the nakedness of your whorings shall be exposed. Your lewdness and your whorings
text.Rotherham: And they shall deal with thee in hatred And take away all thy labour, And leave thee utterly naked,- And thine unchaste shame, shall be disclosed, with thy lewdness and unchastities,

text.NRSV: have brought this upon you, because you played the whore with the nations, and polluted yourself with their idols.
text.Rotherham: These things shall be done to thee, In that thou hast gone unchastely after the nations, Because thou hast defiled thyself with their manufactured gods.

text.NRSV: You have gone the way of your sister; therefore I will give her cup into your hand.
text.Rotherham: In the way of thy sister, hast thou walked, Therefore will I put her cup into thy hand.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: You shall drink your sister’s cup, deep and wide; you shall be scorned and derided, it holds so much.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, The cup of thy sister, shalt thou drink, The cup deep and large, Thou shalt become a laughingstock and a derision, much doth it contain!

text.NRSV: You shall be filled with drunkenness and sorrow. A cup of horror and desolation is the cup of your sister Samaria;
text.Rotherham: With drunkenness and grief, shalt thou be filled,- The cup of astonishment and desolation, The cup of thy sister Samaria;

text.NRSV: you shall drink it and drain it out, and gnaw its sherds, and tear at your breasts; for I have spoken, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: Yea thou shalt drink it and drain it out And the sherds thereof, shalt thou gnaw And thy breasts, shalt thou tear out,- For, I, have spoken, Declareth My Lord. Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God: Because you have forgotten me and cast me behind your back, therefore bear the consequences of your lewdness and whorings.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus, saith My Lord. Yahweh, Because thou hast forgotten me, and has cast me behind thy back, Thou also thyself, bear thou thy lewdness and thine unchaste doings.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to me: Mortal, will you judge Oholah and Oholibah? Then declare to them their abominations.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto me, Son of man Wilt thou judge Oholah and Oholibah? Then declare thou unto them their abominations.

text.NRSV: For they have committed adultery, and blood is on their hands; with their idols they have committed adultery; and they have even offered up to them for food the children whom they had borne to me.
text.Rotherham: For they have committed adultery. And blood, is one their hands, Yea with their manufactured gods, have they committed adultery. And even their own children whom they bare unto me, have they set apart for them to be devoured.

text.NRSV: Moreover they have done this to me: they have defiled my sanctuary on the same day and profaned my sabbaths.
text.Rotherham: Yet more, this, have they done to me, They have defiled my sanctuary, on the same day, And my sabbaths, have they profaned.

text.NRSV: For when they had slaughtered their children for their idols, on the same day they came into my sanctuary to profane it. This is what they did in my house.
text.Rotherham: Yea when they had slaughtered their children to their manufactured gods, then entered they into my sanctuary, on the same day, to profane it,- And lo! thus have they done in the midst of mine own house.

text.NRSV: They even sent for men to come from far away, to whom a messenger was sent, and they came. For them you bathed yourself, painted your eyes, and decked yourself with ornaments;
text.Rotherham: Yea verily, that ye must needs send for men, ready to come in from afar, in that, a messenger, was sent unto them and lo! they came, For whom thou didst bathe thyself Paint thine eyes And deck thyself with ornaments.

text.NRSV: you sat on a stately couch, with a table spread before it on which you had placed my incense and my oil.
text.Rotherham: Then satest thou upon a glorious couch With a table prepared before it,— And mine incense and mine oil, didst thou set thereon.

text.NRSV: The sound of a raucous multitude was around her, with many of the rabble brought from the wilderness; and they put bracelets on the arms of the women, and beautiful crowns upon their heads.
text.Rotherham: The noise of careless throng also, was with her, And besides men out of the mass of mankind, there were brought in drunkards, out of the desert; And they put bracelets upon the hands of both women, And crowns of adorning upon their heads.

text.NRSV: Then I said, Ah, she is worn out with adulteries! Now they will whore with her in spite of all.
text.Rotherham: Then said I of her that was worn out with adulteries, Will they now join in her unchaste doings even hers?

text.NRSV: For they have gone in to her, as one goes in to a whore. Thus they went in to Oholah and to Oholibah, lewd women.
text.Rotherham: Yet came they in unto her, as men go in unto a woman that is a harlot,— So, came they in unto Oholah, and unto Oholibah—the lewd women!

text.NRSV: But righteous animals shall pass on them the judgment of adulteresses, and the judgment of those who shed blood; because they are adulteresses, and blood is on their hands.
text.Rotherham: But righteous men, they, shall judge them, With the judgment of adulteresses, and With the judgment of women that shed blood,— Because adulteresses, they are And blood, is on their hands.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord God: Bring up a mob against them, and make them an object of terror and a spoil.
text.Rotherham: For thus, saith My Lord. Yahweh,- There shall be brought up against them a gathered host, Then shall they be delivered up. for a terror and for a prey,

text.NRSV: The mob shall stone them and with their swords they shall cut them down; they shall kill their sons and their daughters, and burn up their houses.
text.Rotherham: Yea a gathered host shall stone them with stones, and dispatch them with their swords, Their sons and their daughters, shall they slay, And their houses—in the fire, shall they consume.

text.NRSV: Thus will I put an end to lewdness in the land, so that all women may take warning and not commit lewdness as you have done.
text.Rotherham: So will I make an end of lewdness out of the land,- And all women shall be disciplined, and shall not do according to your lewdness.

text.NRSV: They shall repay you for your lewdness, and you shall bear the penalty for your sinful idols; and you shall know that I am the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: Yea the judges Shall set your lewdness upon you, And your sins with manufactured gods, shall ye bear; So shall ye know that, I, am The Lord Yahweh.

text.NRSV: In the ninth year, in the tenth month, on the tenth day of the month, the word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me. in the ninth year in the tenth month, on the tenth of the month, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, write down the name of this day, this very day. The king of Babylon has laid siege to Jerusalem this very day.
text.Rotherham: Son of man. Write thee the name of the day, this selfsame day,—the king of Babylon cast himself against Jerusalem, on this selfsame day.

text.NRSV: And utter an allegory to the rebellious house and say to them, Thus says the Lord God: Set on the pot, set it on, pour in water also;
text.Rotherham: Put thou forth therefore, against the perverse house a parable, and say unto them Thus saith My Lord, Yahweh: Set on the caldron set it on, Moreover also pour into it water:

text.NRSV: put in it the pieces, all the good pieces, the thigh and the shoulder; fill it with choice bones.
text.Rotherham: Gather the pieces thereof into it, Every good piece thigh and shoulder, With the choice of the bones, fill it:

text.NRSV: Take the choicest of the flock, pile the logs under it; boil its pieces, seethe also its bones in it.
text.Rotherham: Take of the choice of the rock, Moreover also pile of bones place under it,- Boil the pieces thereof, Yea let the bones of it seethe in the midst thereof.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God: Woe to the bloody city, to the pot whose rust is in it, whose rust has not gone out of it! Take out of it piece after piece; no lot has fallen on it.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, Woe! city of bloodshed. The caldron whose scum is in it, and whose scum, hath not gone out of it: Piece by piece, bring it out, There hath fallen thereon no lot.

text.NRSV: For the blood she shed is inside it; she placed it on the bare rock; she did not pour it out on the ground, to cover it with earth.
text.Rotherham: For, her own blood, hath come to be in her midst, Upon the smooth face of the cliff, hath she set it,- She hath not poured it out on the earth, that she might cover it with dust.

text.NRSV: To rouse my wrath, to take vengeance, I have placed the blood she shed on the bare rock, so that it may not be covered.
text.Rotherham: To bring up indignation to execute an avenging, have I set her blood upon the smooth face of the cliff,—that it may not be covered.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God: Woe to the bloody city! I will even make the pile great.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Woe! city of bloodshed,—Even I myself, will make large the pile.

text.NRSV: Heap up the logs, kindle the fire; boil the meat well, mix in the spices, and let the bones be burned.
text.Rotherham: Heap on the wood Kindle the fire, Consume the flesh, Yea season with spice, And let the bones be scorched.

text.NRSV: Then set it empty upon the coals, so that it may become hot, and its copper glow, so that its filth may be melted in it, its rust consumed.
text.Rotherham: Then set it upon the live coals thereof empty,—that the copper of it may be scorched and burn. So that the impurity thereof may be annihilated within it The scum thereof be consumed,

text.NRSV: In vain I have wearied myself; its thick rust does not depart. To the fire with its rust!
text.Rotherham: With toils, hath she wearied herself,—Since her abundant scum will not go out of her, into the fire, with her scum!

text.NRSV: Because of your filthy lewdness, because I would have cleansed you and you were not cleansed from your filthiness, you shall not be cleansed any more until I have satisfied my fury upon you.
text.Rotherham: In thine uncleanness, is lewdness; be- cause I purified thee and thou wast not purified, from thine uncleanness, thou shalt not be purified any more, until I have let mine indignation rest upon thee.

text.NRSV: I the Lord have spoken; the time is coming, I will act. I will not refrain, I will not spare, I will not relent. According to your ways and your deeds you shall be judged, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: I Yahweh, have spoken It cometh and I will perform I will not let alone, neither will I shield, Nor will I repent,- According to thy ways and according to thy doings, have they judged thee, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, with one blow I am about to take away from you the delight of your eyes; yet you shall not mourn or weep, nor shall your tears run down.
text.Rotherham: Son of Man Behold me! taking away from thee the delight of thine eyes, with a stroke,- But thou shall not lament Neither shalt thou weep, Neither shall come—thy tears:

text.NRSV: Sigh, but not aloud; make no mourning for the dead. Bind on your turban, and put your sandals on your feet; do not cover your upper lip or eat the bread of mourners.
text.Rotherham: To groan, forbear, Over the dead—no mourning, shalt thou make Thy chaplet, bind thou on thee And thy sandals:, put thou on thy feet, And thou shalt not cover thy beard, And the bread of other men, shalt thou not eat.

text.NRSV: So I spoke to the people in the morning, and at evening my wife died. And on the next morning I did as I was commanded.
text.Rotherham: So I spake unto the people in the morning, and my wife died in the evening,—and I did in the morning, as I had been commanded.

text.NRSV: Then the people said to me, “Will you not tell us what these things mean for us, that you are acting this way?”
text.Rotherham: Then said the people unto me: Wilt thou not tell us what these things are to us, that thou shouldst be acting thus?

text.NRSV: Then I said to them: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: So I said unto them: The word of Yahweh hath come unto me, saying:

text.NRSV: Say to the house of Israel, Thus says the Lord God: I will profane my sanctuary, the pride of your power, the delight of your eyes, and your heart’s desire; and your sons and your daughters whom you left behind shall fall by the sword.
text.Rotherham: Say to the house of Israel thus, saith My Lord. Yahweh, Behold me! profaning my sanctuary. The pride of your strength, The delight of your eyes And the object of the tender regard of your soul,- And your sons and your daughters whom ye have left behind, by the sword, shall fall.

text.NRSV: And you shall do as I have done; you shall not cover your upper lip or eat the bread of mourners.
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye do, as I have done.— The beard, shall ye not cover, And the bread of other men:, shall ye not eat;

text.NRSV: Your turbans shall be on your heads and your sandals on your feet; you shall not mourn or weep, but you shall pine away in your iniquities and groan to one another.
text.Rotherham: And your chaplets shall be on your heads. And your sandals on your feet, Ye shall neither lament, nor weep,- Yet shall ye pine away in your iniquities, and shall groan one to another.

text.NRSV: Thus Ezekiel shall be a sign to you; you shall do just as he has done. When this comes, then you shall know that I am the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: So shall Ezekiel become to you a wonder, According to all that he hath done, shall ye do: When it cometh, then shall ye know that I am The Lord, Yahweh.

text.NRSV: And you, mortal, on the day when I take from them their stronghold, their joy and glory, the delight of their eyes and their heart’s affection, and also their sons and their daughters,
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore Son of man, Shall it not be— In the day when I take away from them Their strength, The joy of their adorning,— The delight of their eyes And the desire of their soul, Their sons and their daughters

text.NRSV: on that day, one who has escaped will come to you to report to you the news.
text.Rotherham: That in that day, one that hath escaped shall come in unto thee,—to cause thee to hear it with thine own ears?

text.NRSV: On that day your mouth shall be opened to the one who has escaped, and you shall speak and no longer be silent. So you shall be a sign to them; and they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: That in that day, thy mouth shall be opened unto him that hath escaped, And thou shalt speak and not be dumb any longer? So shalt thou become to them a wonder, And they shall know that, I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, set your face toward the Ammonites and prophesy against them.
text.Rotherham: Son of man Set thy face against the sons of Ammon,— and prophesy against them.

text.NRSV: Say to the Ammonites, Hear the word of the Lord God: Thus says the Lord God, Because you said, “Aha!” over my sanctuary when it was profaned, and over the land of Israel when it was made desolate, and over the house of Judah when it went into exile;
text.Rotherham: So then thou shalt say to the sons of Ammon, Hear ye the word of My Lord Yahweh: Thus, saith My Lord. Yahweh: Because thou saidst Aha! Against my sanctuary when it was profaned. And against the soil of Israel when it was laid waste, And against the house of Judah when they went into exile.

text.NRSV: therefore I am handed you over to the people of the east for a possession. They shall set their encampments among you and pitch their tents in your midst; they shall eat your fruit, and they shall drink your milk.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, behold me! delivering thee up to the Sons of the East for a possession And they shall set their encampments in thee, And place in thee their habitations, They, shall eat thy fruits, And they, shall drink thy milk;

text.NRSV: I will make Rabbah a pasture for camels and Ammon a fold for flocks. Then you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And I will make of Rabbah a home for camels, And the sons of Ammon a couching-place for flocks, So shall ye know that, I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord God: Because you have clapped your hands and stamped your feet and rejoiced with all the malice within you against the land of Israel,
text.Rotherham: For, thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Because thou didst clap thy hands and stamp with thy foot,—and didst rejoice with all thy contempt in thy soul, against the soil of Israel.

text.NRSV: therefore I have stretched out my hand against you, and will hand you over as spoil to the nations; I will cut you off from the peoples and will make you perish out of the countries; I will destroy you. Then you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Therefore behold me! I have stretched out my hand over thee And will deliver thee for a prey to the nations, And will cut thee off from among the peoples, And will cause thee to perish from among the lands,— I will destroy thee, So shalt thou know that, I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: Because Moab and Seir said, “See, the house of Judah is like all the other nations,”
text.Rotherham: Thus saith My Lord Yahweh: Because Moab and Seir say, Lo! like all the nations, is the house of Judah.

text.NRSV: therefore I will lay open the flank of Moab from the towns on its frontier, Beth-jeshimoth, Baal-meon, and Kiriathaim, the glory of the country.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, behold me! laying open the side of Moab out of the cities, Out of his cities on his frontiers, The beauty of the land of Beth-jeshimoth, Baal-meon, and Kiriathaim:

text.NRSV: I will give it along with the Ammonites to the people of the east as a possession, so that it may not be remembered among the nations.
text.Rotherham: To the Sons of the East [when they come] against the sons of Ammon, and I will deliver it up for possession,-to the end the sons of Ammon may not be remembered among the nations:

text.NRSV: Thus I will execute judgments upon Moab. Then they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Also upon Moab, will I execute judgments,- So shall they know that I, am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: Because Edom acted revengefully against the house of Judah and has incurred great guilt by taking revenge on them,
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith My Lord. Yahweh, Because of what Edom hath done, in taking vengeance on the house of Judah, so that they have become guilty again and again and have taken vengeance upon them,

text.NRSV: therefore thus says the Lord God, I will stretch out my hand against Edom, and cut off from it humans and animals, and I will make it waste; from Teman even to Dedan they shall fall by the sword.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, I will therefore stretch forth my hand over Edom, and will cut off therefrom man and beast, And will deliver it up as a desolation, from Teman, And they of Dedan, by the sword, shall fall,

text.NRSV: I will lay my vengeance upon Edom by the hand of my people Israel; and they shall act in Edom according to my anger and according to my fury; and they shall know my vengeance, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: And I will put forth mine avenging against Edom, by the hand of my people Israel, And they shall deal with Edom, according to mine anger and according to mine indignation,- So shall they know mine avenging, Declareth My Lord. Yahweh,

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: Because with unending hostilities the Philistines acted in vengeance, and with malice of heart took revenge in destruction;
text.Rotherham: Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, Because of what the Philistines have done by way of vengeance,- In that they have taken vengeance with contempt in the soul, to destroy, with the enmity of age-past times.

text.NRSV: therefore thus says the Lord God, I will stretch out my hand against the Philistines, cut off the Cherethites, and destroy the rest of the seacoast.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus, saith My Lord. Yahweh, Behold me! stretching forth my hand over the Philistines, And I will cut off the Cherethim,— And will cause to perish the remnant of the coast of the sea;

text.NRSV: I will execute great vengeance on them with fiery rebukes. Then they shall know that I am the Lord, when I lay my vengeance on them.
text.Rotherham: And I will execute upon them great vengeance, with rebukes of indignation: shall they know that I am Yahweh, when I put forth mine avenging against them.

text.NRSV: In the eleventh year, on the first day of the month, the word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass in the eleventh year, on the first of the month, that the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, because Tyre said concerning Jerusalem, “Aha, the gate of the peoples is broken, it has swung open to me; I shall be replenished, now that she is laid waste,”
text.Rotherham: Son of man, Because Tyre hath said concerning Jerusalem. Aha! She is broken that was the doors of the peoples. She is turned unto me,— I shall be filled, She is laid waste,

text.NRSV: therefore, thus says the Lord God: See, I am against you, O Tyre! I will hurl many nations against you, as the sea hurls its waves.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Behold me! against thee O Tyre,— So then I will bring up against thee many nations, As the sea bringeth up its rolling waves;

text.NRSV: They shall destroy the walls of Tyre and break down her towers. I will scrape her soil from her and make her a bare rock.
text.Rotherham: And they shall destroy the walls of Tyre. And break down her towers, And I will scrape clean her dust from off her, —And make of her the glaring face of a cliff:

text.NRSV: She shall become a place for spreading nets in the midst of the sea; for I have spoken, says the Lord God. She shall become spoil for the nations;
text.Rotherham: A place for the spreading of nets, shall she become, in the midst of the sea, For, I, have spoken Declareth My Lord. Yahweh,-And she shall become a prey to the nations.

text.NRSV: and her daughter-towns in the mainland shall be killed by the sword. Then they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And her daughters that are in the field: with the sword shall be slain: So shall they know that I am Yahweh.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord God: I will bring against Tyre from the north King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon, king of kings, with horses and chariots and cavalry and a great army.
text.Rotherham: For thus saith My Lord. Yahweh, Behold me! bringing against Tyre. Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon out of the North. a king of kings,—with horses and with chariots and with horsemen, and a gathered host, and much people.

text.NRSV: Your daughter-towns in the mainland he shall put to the sword. He shall set up a siege-wall against you, cast up a mound against you, and raise a roof of shields against you.
text.Rotherham: Thy daughters in the field—with the sword, shall he slay,- And shall set against thee a siege-wall And cast up against thee an earthwork, And set up against thee a large shield;

text.NRSV: He shall direct the shock of his battering rams against your walls and break down your towers with his axes.
text.Rotherham: And the stroke of his attacking-engine, will he direct against thy walls,— And thy towers, will he break down with his axes.

text.NRSV: His horses shall be so many that their dust shall cover you. At the noise of cavalry, wheels, and chariots your very walls shall shake, when he enters your gates as one enters a city that has been breached.
text.Rotherham: By reason of the multitude of his horses, their dust shall cover thee,— At the noise of horseman and wheel and chariot, shall thy walls tremble, when he entereth into thy gates Just as they do who enter a city broken open.

text.NRSV: With the hoofs of his horses he shall trample all your streets. He shall put your people to the sword, and your strong pillars shall fall to the ground.
text.Rotherham: With the hoofs of his horses, shall he tread down all thy streets,— Thy people-with the sword, shall he slay, And thy pillars of strength to the earth, shall go down.

text.NRSV: They shall plunder your riches and loot your merchandise; they shall break down your walls and destroy your fine houses. Your stones and timber and soil they shall cast into the water.
text.Rotherham: And they shall spoil thy wealth And make a prey of thy merchandise, And break down thy walls And thy pleasant houses, shall they lay in ruins; And thy stones and thy timber and thy dust—in the midst of the waters, shall they lay.

text.NRSV: I will silence the music of your songs; the sound of your lyres shall be heard no more.
text.Rotherham: And I will cause to cease the hum of thy songs; And the sound of thy lyres, shall not be heard any more.

text.NRSV: I will make you a bare rock; you shall be a place for spreading nets. You shall never again be rebuilt, for I the Lord have spoken, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: And I will make of thee the glaring face of a cliff. A place for the spreading of nets, shalt thou become, Thou shalt not be but any more, For I. Yahweh have spoken it, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God to Tyre: Shall not the coastlands shake at the sound of your fall, when the wounded groan, when slaughter goes on within you?
text.Rotherham: Thus saith My Lord. Yahweh, to Tyre: Shall it not be that. At the sound of thy fall. With the groan of thy pierced one. With the slaughter made in thy midst, The Coastlands shall tremble?

text.NRSV: Then all the princes of the sea shall step down from their thrones; they shall remove their robes and strip off their embroidered garments. They shall clothe themselves with trembling, and shall sit on the ground; they shall tremble every moment, and be appalled at you.
text.Rotherham: Then shall come down from off their thrones all the princes of the sea, And shall lay aside their robes. And their embroidered garments, shall strip off,— With tremblings, shall they clothe them. selves Upon the ground, shall they sit, And shall tremble every moment and be astonished over thee.

text.NRSV: And they shall raise a lamentation over you, and say to you: How you have vanished from the seas, O city of renown, once mighty on the sea, you and your inhabitants, who imposed your terror on all the mainland!
text.Rotherham: Then shall they take up over thee a dirge, And say to thee,- How hast thou perished, And ceased from the seas,- The city renowned which was strong in the sea She and they who dwelt in her, Who imparted their terror To all who dwelt in her.

text.NRSV: Now the coastlands tremble on the day of your fall; the islands in the sea are dismayed at your passing.
text.Rotherham: Now, shall tremble the Coastlands, In the day of thy fall,- And shall shudder the isles that are in the sea. At thine exit!

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord God: When I make you a desolate city, like the cities that are not inhabited, when I bring up the deep over you, and the great waters cover you,
text.Rotherham: For thus saith My Lord Yahweh, When I make thee a desolate city, Like cities which cannot be dwelt in,- When I bring up over thee the roaring deep, And the mighty waters cover thee,

text.NRSV: then I will thrust you down with those who descend into the Pit, to the people of long ago, and I will make you live in the world below, among ancient ruins, with those who go down to the Pit, so that you will not be inhabited and have a place in the land of the living.
text.Rotherham: Then will I bring thee down with them that go down into the pit. Unto the people of age-past times And cause thee to dwell in the earth below Among the desolations from age-past times With them that go down into the pit That thou mayest not be dwelt in, Nor yet present thyself in the land of the living.

text.NRSV: I will bring you to a dreadful end, and you shall be no more; though sought for, you will never be found again, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: A terror, will I make thee And thou shalt not be: Though thou be sought for, Thou shalt not be found any more To times age-abiding, Declareth My Lord, Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying:

text.NRSV: Now you, mortal, raise a lamentation over Tyre,
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, son of man, Take up over Tyre a dirge;

text.NRSV: and say to Tyre, which sits at the entrance to the sea, merchant of the peoples on many coastlands, Thus says the Lord God: O Tyre, you have said, “I am perfect in beauty.”
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt say unto Tyre, O thou that dwellest by the entrances of the sea, Thou merchant of the peoples unto many shores,— Thus, saith My Lord, Yahweh, O Tyre, Thou saidst, I, am the perfection of beauty!

text.NRSV: Your borders are in the heart of the seas; your builders made perfect your beauty.
text.Rotherham: In the heart of the seas, were thy bounds, —Thy builders perfected thy beauty:

text.NRSV: They made all your planks of fir trees from Senir; they took a cedar from Lebanon to make a mast for you.
text.Rotherham: With fir-trees from Senir, but they for thee all the two-decked vessels,— Cedar from Lebanon, fetched they, to make masts for thee:

text.NRSV: Of oaks of Bashan they made your oars; they made your deck of pines from the coasts of Cyprus, inlaid with ivory.
text.Rotherham: With oaks from Bashan, made they thine oars,— Thy benches, made they of ivory, inlaid with boxwood, From the shores of the West:

text.NRSV: Of fine embroidered linen from Egypt was your sail, serving as your ensign; blue and purple from the coasts of Elishah was your awning.
text.Rotherham: Of fine linen with embroidered work from Egypt, was thy sail, To serve thee for ensign,— Blue and purple from the shores of Aeolis became thine awning:

text.NRSV: The inhabitants of Sidon and Arvad were your rowers; skilled men of Zemer were within you, they were your pilots.
text.Rotherham: the inhabitants of Zidon and Arvad, Became rowers for thee,— Thine own skilled men O Tyre, were within thee, They were thy sailors:

text.NRSV: The elders of Gebal and its skilled men were within you, caulking your seams; all the ships of the sea with their mariners were within you, to barter for your wares.
text.Rotherham: the elders of Byblus and the skilled men thereof, were within thee, Thy calkers,— All the ships of the sea and their mariners, were within thee, To carry on thy traffic,

text.NRSV: Persia and Lud and Put were in your army as your warriors; they hung shield and helmet in you; they gave you splendor.
text.Rotherham: They of Persia and Lud and Phut were in thine army, Thy men of war,— Shield and helmet, hung they up within thee, They set forth thy splendour:

text.NRSV: Men of Arvad and Helech were on your walls all around; men of Gamad were in your towers. They hung their quivers all around your walls; they made perfect your beauty.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Arvad, :with thine army, were upon thy walls round about, And valorous in thy towers,— Their shields, hung they up on thy walls round about, They perfected thy beauty:

text.NRSV: Tarshish did business with you because of your great wealth of every kind; silver, iron, tin, and lead they exchanged for your wares.
text.Rotherham: Tarshish was a trader of thine. For the abundance of all wealth,— With silver iron. tin and lead, Took they part in thy traffic:

text.NRSV: Javan, Tubal, and Meshech traded with you; they exchanged human beings and vessels of bronze for your merchandise.
text.Rotherham: Javan. Tubal and Meshech, They were merchants of thine,— With the persons of men and vessels of bronze, Shared they in thy barter:

text.NRSV: Beth-togarmah exchanged for your wares horses, war horses, and mules.
text.Rotherham: Some of the house of Togarmah, with horses and horsemen and mules, Took part in thy traffic:

text.NRSV: The Rhodians traded with you; many coastlands were your stockholders; ivory tusks and ebony they brought you in payment.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Dedan were merchants of thine, Many, isles, took the merchandise of thy hand, —Horns of ivory and ebony, gave they back to thee in exchange:

text.NRSV: Edom did business with you because of your many goods; they exchanged for your wares turquoise, purple, embroidered work, fine linen, coral, and rubies.
text.Rotherham: Syria, was a trader of thine For the abundance of thy manufactures,- With carbuncle and purple and embroidery and fine linen and coral and rubies, Took they part in thy traffic:

text.NRSV: Judah and the land of Israel traded with you; they exchanged for your merchandise wheat from Minnith, millet, honey, oil, and balm.
text.Rotherham: Judah and the land of Israel, They, were merchants of thine,— With the wheat of Minnith and the sweets of pannag and honey and oil and balsam, Shared they in thy barter:

text.NRSV: Damascus signaled business with you because of your many goods—because of your great wealth of every kind—wine of Helbon, and white wool.
text.Rotherham: Damascus was a trader of thine For the multitude of thy manufactures For the multitude of all wealth,- With the wine of Helbon and white wool:

text.NRSV: Vedan and Javan from Uzal entered into trade for your wares; wrought iron, cassia, and sweet cane were bartered for your merchandise.
text.Rotherham: Wedan and Javan, from Uzal, Brought into thy traffic,— Steel, cassia and calamus, Were, in thy merchandise:

text.NRSV: Dedan traded with you in saddlecloths for riding.
text.Rotherham: Dedan was a merchant of thine,- With spreading wraps for riding:

text.NRSV: Arabia and all the princes of Kedar were your favored dealers in lambs, rams, and goats; in these they did business with you.
text.Rotherham: Arabia and all the princes of Kedar, They, were traders at thy service,— With lambs and rams and he-goats, With these, were they traders of thine:

text.NRSV: The merchants of Sheba and Raamah traded with you; they exchanged for your wares the best of all kinds of spices, and all precious stones, and gold.
text.Rotherham: The merchants of Sheba and Raamah, They were merchants of thine,— With the chief of all spices and with every kind of precious stone and gold, Took they part in thy traffic:

text.NRSV: Haran, Canneh, Eden, the merchants of Sheba, Asshur, and Chilmad traded with you.
text.Rotherham: Haran and Canneh and Eden, the merchants of Sheba,—Asshur. Chilmad, Were merchants of thine:

text.NRSV: These traded with you in choice garments, in clothes of blue and embroidered work, and in carpets of colored material, bound with cords and made secure; in these they traded with you.
text.Rotherham: Those were thy merchants for gorgeous garments, for wrappings of blue and embroidery, and for treasure-chests of variegated cloth,—for cords, twisted and strong, in thy market:

text.NRSV: The ships of Tarshish carried your merchandise. So you were filled and made very glorious in the heart of the seas.
text.Rotherham: the ships of Tarshish, were thy masts Carrying on thy barter, So wast thou filled and becamest very glorious In the heart of the seas.

text.NRSV: Your rowers have brought you into the high seas. The east wind has wrecked you in the heart of the seas.
text.Rotherham: Into mighty waters, have they brought thee, the rowers—even thee,— the east wind, hath broken thee In the heart of the seas:

text.NRSV: Your riches, your wares, your merchandise, your mariners, and your pilots, your caulkers, your dealers in merchandise, and all your warriors within you, with all the company that is with you, sink into the heart of the seas on the day of your ruin.
text.Rotherham: Thy wealth and thy wares, thy merchandise thy mariners, and thy sailors,-thy calkers and the barterers of thy merchandise and all thy men of war who are in thee and in all thy gathered host which is in thy midst Shall fall into the heart of the seas, In the day of thy fall.

text.NRSV: At the sound of the cry of your pilots the countryside shakes,
text.Rotherham: At the sound of the outcry of thy pilots, the coasts shall quake:

text.NRSV: and down from their ships come all that handle the oar. The mariners and all the pilots of the sea stand on the shore
text.Rotherham: Then shall come down from their ships all that handle the oar, mariners, all the sailors of the sea— Upon the land, shall they take up their station;

text.NRSV: and wail aloud over you, and cry bitterly. They throw dust on their heads and wallow in ashes;
text.Rotherham: And make loud [lament] over thee, with their voice, And make biter outcry,- And shall lift up dust on their heads, In ashes, shall roll themselves;

text.NRSV: they make themselves bald for you, and put on sackcloth, and they weep over you in bitterness of soul, with bitter mourning.
text.Rotherham: And shall make bald for thee a baldness And gird them with sackcloth,— And shall weep for thee in bitterness of soul A bitter lamentation;

text.NRSV: In their wailing they raise a lamentation for you, and lament over you: “Who was ever destroyed like Tyre in the midst of the sea?”
text.Rotherham: And shall take up for thee in their Wailing: a dirge, And shall chant over thee,- Who is like Tyre? Like the Sent One in the midst of the sea?

text.NRSV: When your wares came from the seas, you satisfied many peoples; with your abundant wealth and merchandise you enriched the kings of the earth.
text.Rotherham: By the going forth of thy commodities out of the seas, Thou didst satisfy many peoples, By the multitude of thy wares and of thy merchandise, Thou didst enrich the kings of the earth.

text.NRSV: Now you are wrecked by the seas, in the depths of the waters; your merchandise and all your company have sunk with you.
text.Rotherham: Now, thou art wrecked out of the seas In the depths of waters,— Thy merchandise and all thy gathered host In thy midst, have fallen.

text.NRSV: All the inhabitants of the coastlands are appalled at you; and their kings are horribly afraid, their faces are convulsed.
text.Rotherham: All the dwellers in the coastlands, Are astounded over thee,- And their kings have been made to shudder a shuddering, To be troubled in countenance.

text.NRSV: The merchants among the peoples hiss at you; you have come to a dreadful end and shall be no more forever.
text.Rotherham: The traders among the peoples, have hissed over thee: A terror, hast thou become, And, art not, Unto times age-abiding.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying

text.NRSV: Mortal, say to the prince of Tyre, Thus says the Lord God: Because your heart is proud and you have said, “I am a god; I sit in the seat of the gods, in the heart of the seas,” yet you are but a mortal, and no god, though you compare your mind with the mind of a god.
text.Rotherham: Son of man Say to the Prince of Tyre. Thus, saith My Lord. Yahweh— Because lofty, is thy heart and thou hast said A GOD, am I, In the seat of God, have I taken my seat In the heart of the seas; Whereas thou art a man, and not a GOD, But hast set thy heart as the heart of God:

text.NRSV: You are indeed wiser than Daniel; no secret is hidden from you;
text.Rotherham: Lo! wiser, thou than Daniel,— No secret, have they hidden from thee:

text.NRSV: by your wisdom and your understanding you have amassed wealth for yourself, and have gathered gold and silver into your treasuries.
text.Rotherham: In thy wisdom and in thine understanding, hast thou gotten thee wealth,— And hast gotten gold and silver into thy treasuries:

text.NRSV: By your great skill in trade you have increased your wealth, and your heart has become proud in your wealth.
text.Rotherham: By the greatness of thy wisdom and by thy traffic, hast thou multiplied thy riches, -And thy heart hath become lofty in thy riches:

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God: Because you compare your mind with the mind of a god,
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus saith My Lord Yahweh,— Because thou hast set thy heart as the heart of God,

text.NRSV: therefore, I will bring strangers against you, the most terrible of the nations; they shall draw their swords against the beauty of your wisdom and defile your splendor.
text.Rotherham: Therefore behold me! bringing upon thee aliens, The terrible ones of the nations,- And they shall unsheathe their swords over the beauty of thy wisdom, And profane thy splendour:

text.NRSV: They shall thrust you down into the Pit, and you shall die a violent death in the heart of the seas.
text.Rotherham: To the pit, shall they take thee down,- And thou shalt die the deaths of one thrust through, In the heart of the seas.

text.NRSV: Will you still say, “I am a god,” in the presence of those who kill you, though you are but a mortal, and no god, in the hands of those who wound you?
text.Rotherham: Wilt thou really say, God, am I, before him who is slaying thee; When, thou, art a man and not a God, in the hand of them who are piercing thee?

text.NRSV: You shall die the death of the uncircumcised by the hand of strangers; for I have spoken, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: The deaths of the uncircumcised, shalt thou die by the hand of aliens,— For I have spoken, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Moreover the word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, raise a lamentation over the king of Tyre, and say to him, Thus says the Lord God: You were the signet of perfection, full of wisdom and perfect in beauty.
text.Rotherham: Son of man Take thou up a dirge over the king of Tyre,— And thou shalt say to him Thus saith My Lord. Yahweh, Thou wast of finished proportions, Full of wisdom and Perfect in beauty:

text.NRSV: You were in Eden, the garden of God; every precious stone was your covering, carnelian, chrysolite, and moonstone, beryl, onyx, and jasper, sapphire, turquoise, and emerald; and worked in gold were your settings and your engravings. On the day that you were created they were prepared.
text.Rotherham: In Eden the garden of God, thou wast. Of every precious stone, was thy covering-Sardius. topaz, and diamond, Chrysolite beryl, and jasper, Sapphire carbuncle and emerald,- And of gold, was the work of thy timbrels and thy flutes within thee, In the day thou wast created, were they prepared:

text.NRSV: With an anointed cherub as guardian I placed you; you were on the holy mountain of God; you walked among the stones of fire.
text.Rotherham: Thou, wast the anointed cherub that covered,— When I appointed thee, in the holy mount of God, thou wast, Amid stones of fire, thou didst walk to and fro:

text.NRSV: You were blameless in your ways from the day that you were created, until iniquity was found in you.
text.Rotherham: Complete, wast thou in thy ways, from the day thou wast created,- Until perversity was found in thee.

text.NRSV: In the abundance of your trade you were filled with violence, and you sinned; so I cast you as a profane thing from the mountain of God, and the guardian cherub drove you out from among the stones of fire.
text.Rotherham: By the abundance of thy traffic, they filled thy midst with violence And thou didst sin,- So I cast thee as profane out of the mountain of God. And destroyed thee O covering cherub, from amid the stones of fire:

text.NRSV: Your heart was proud because of your beauty; you corrupted your wisdom for the sake of your splendor. I cast you to the ground; I exposed you before kings, to feast their eyes on you.
text.Rotherham: Lofty, was thy heart in thy beauty, Thou didst corrupt thy wisdom, because of thy splendour,— Upon the earth, did I cast thee. Before kings, did I set thee That they might look at thee:

text.NRSV: By the multitude of your iniquities, in the unrighteousness of your trade, you profaned your sanctuaries. So I brought out fire from within you; it consumed you, and I turned you to ashes on the ground in the sight of all who saw you.
text.Rotherham: Owing to the abounding of thine iniquities. In the perversity of thy traffic, Thou didst profane thy sanctuaries, Therefore brought I forth fire out of thy midst. the same devoured thee, And I turned thee to ashes on the ground, Before the eyes of all beholding thee:

text.NRSV: All who know you among the peoples are appalled at you; you have come to a dreadful end and shall be no more forever.
text.Rotherham: All that had known thee among the peoples were astounded over thee,— A terror, hast thou become, And, art not, Unto times age-abiding.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, set your face toward Sidon, and prophesy against it,
text.Rotherham: Son of man, Set thy face towards Zidon,— And prophesy over it;
